products.name: pressure_zone
products.description: Master record defining hydraulic pressure zones within the distribution network. Captures zone name, target operating pressure range (PSI min/max), design pressure, elevation range, boundary description, associated storage facilities, PRV stations controlling the zone, and Innovyze InfoWater hydraulic model zone reference. Used for pressure management, NRW analysis, and DMA boundary definition.
products.reference: Innovyze InfoWater pressure zone; AWWA M32 Distribution Network Analysis

attributes.name: pressure_zone_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the pressure zone within the distribution network. Primary key.

attributes.name: compliance_permit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Pressure zones are operational units subject to permit conditions for minimum pressure requirements, water quality monitoring locations, and disinfection residual compliance. Permit conditions specify

attributes.name: primary_storage_facility_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the primary storage tank or reservoir that serves this pressure zone, providing hydraulic head and emergency storage.

attributes.name: arcgis_feature_code
attributes.description: Corresponding feature identifier in the Esri ArcGIS Geographic Information System (GIS) for spatial representation and network topology management.

attributes.name: average_daily_demand_mgd
attributes.description: Average daily water demand in Million Gallons per Day (MGD) for the pressure zone, used for capacity planning and hydraulic modeling.

attributes.name: average_elevation_ft
attributes.description: Average ground elevation in feet above sea level across the pressure zone, used for hydraulic modeling and demand allocation.

attributes.name: boundary_description
attributes.description: Textual description of the geographic or infrastructure boundaries defining the pressure zone, including major streets, landmarks, or infrastructure features.

attributes.name: commissioning_date
attributes.description: Date when the pressure zone was officially commissioned and placed into active service for water distribution operations.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this pressure zone record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: customer_count
attributes.description: Total number of active customer service connections within the pressure zone, used for demand forecasting and revenue allocation.

attributes.name: design_pressure_psi
attributes.description: Design or nominal operating pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) for which the zone infrastructure was engineered and constructed.

attributes.name: elevation_max_ft
attributes.description: Maximum ground elevation in feet above sea level within the pressure zone boundary, critical for pressure management and PRV settings.

attributes.name: elevation_min_ft
attributes.description: Minimum ground elevation in feet above sea level within the pressure zone boundary, used for hydraulic gradient calculations.

attributes.name: fire_flow_capacity_gpm
attributes.description: Minimum fire flow capacity in Gallons per Minute (GPM) that the pressure zone must maintain at specified residual pressure for fire protection, per Insurance Services Office (ISO) and NFPA standards.

attributes.name: hydraulic_model_last_calibrated_date
attributes.description: Date when the hydraulic model for this pressure zone was last calibrated against field measurements, ensuring model accuracy for planning and operational decisions.

attributes.name: infowater_model_zone_code
attributes.description: Corresponding pressure zone identifier in the Innovyze InfoWater hydraulic model, used for synchronization between operational systems and modeling platforms.

attributes.name: last_boundary_review_date
attributes.description: Date when the pressure zone boundaries were last reviewed and validated for accuracy, typically as part of GIS updates or hydraulic model recalibration.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this pressure zone record was last modified, used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for operational notes, special considerations, historical context, or other relevant information about the pressure zone.

attributes.name: nrw_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of Non-Revenue Water (NRW) within the pressure zone, calculated as the difference between water supplied and billed consumption, used for loss control and efficiency analysis.

attributes.name: operational_status
attributes.description: Current operational state of the pressure zone indicating whether it is actively serving customers, temporarily inactive, under maintenance, in emergency mode, or in planned development.

attributes.name: peak_hour_demand_mgd
attributes.description: Peak hourly water demand in Million Gallons per Day (MGD) for the pressure zone, critical for sizing infrastructure and ensuring adequate pressure during high-demand periods.

attributes.name: residual_pressure_fire_psi
attributes.description: Minimum residual pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) that must be maintained during fire flow conditions to ensure adequate service to other customers and fire suppression effectiveness.

attributes.name: scada_zone_tag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Zone Tag
attributes.description: SCADA system tag or point identifier for real-time monitoring of pressure, flow, and operational status within this zone via OSIsoft PI Historian or similar SCADA platforms.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Zone Tag

attributes.name: service_area_sq_mi
attributes.description: Geographic area in square miles covered by the pressure zone, used for demand density calculations and infrastructure planning.

attributes.name: storage_capacity_mg
attributes.description: Total storage capacity in Million Gallons (MG) of all tanks and reservoirs serving the pressure zone, used for emergency supply and pressure stabilization.

attributes.name: target_pressure_max_psi
attributes.description: Maximum target operating pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) to prevent infrastructure damage, excessive leakage, and customer service issues.

attributes.name: target_pressure_min_psi
attributes.description: Minimum target operating pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) that must be maintained throughout the zone to ensure adequate service delivery and regulatory compliance.

attributes.name: ufw_percentage
attributes.description: Percentage of Unaccounted-for Water (UFW) within the pressure zone, representing water losses that cannot be attributed to known uses, critical for leak detection and infrastructure assessment.

attributes.name: zone_code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code or identifier for the pressure zone used in GIS, SCADA, and hydraulic modeling systems.

attributes.name: zone_name
attributes.description: Business name or designation of the pressure zone used for operational reference and communication.

attributes.name: zone_type
attributes.description: Classification of the pressure zone based on the primary method of pressure maintenance: gravity-fed from elevated storage, pumped from pump stations, combination of both, elevated tank-fed, or booster zone.

products.name: dma
products.description: Master record for District Metered Areas (DMAs) — discrete, hydraulically isolated zones of the distribution network used for NRW/UFW monitoring and leakage management. Captures DMA name, boundary description, inlet meter points, outlet meter points, target NRW percentage, minimum night flow (MNF) threshold (GPM), associated pressure zone, GIS polygon boundary, and active status. Core to leakage detection and water loss control programs.
products.reference: IWA District Metered Area; AWWA M36 Water Audits and Loss Control

attributes.name: dma_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the District Metered Area. Primary key for the DMA master record.

attributes.name: pressure_zone_id
attributes.description: Reference to the pressure zone within which this DMA operates. Pressure zones define areas of similar hydraulic pressure managed by Pressure Reducing Valves (PRVs) and pump stations.

attributes.name: average_pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Average Pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)
attributes.description: Average operating pressure within the DMA measured in Pounds per Square Inch. Pressure management is critical for leakage control; excessive pressure increases leak rates and pipe stress.

tag_set.value: Average Pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)

attributes.name: boundary_description
attributes.description: Textual description of the DMA boundary including street names, landmarks, and physical boundaries used to define the hydraulically isolated zone.

attributes.name: code
attributes.description: Business identifier code for the DMA used in operational systems, GIS, and reporting. Typically alphanumeric and unique across the distribution network.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the DMA record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: criticality_rating
attributes.description: Business criticality rating of the DMA based on factors such as population served, infrastructure condition, leakage history, and service area importance. Critical DMAs receive priority for monitoring and maintenance.

attributes.name: decommissioned_date
attributes.description: Date when the DMA was decommissioned or reconfigured. Null for active DMAs. Used for historical tracking and audit purposes.

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Detailed description of the DMA including boundary landmarks, service area characteristics, and any operational notes relevant to leakage management and monitoring.

attributes.name: design_flow_mgd
attributes.business_glossary_term: Design Flow in Million Gallons per Day (MGD)
attributes.description: Design flow capacity for the DMA in Million Gallons per Day. Represents the maximum daily demand the DMA is engineered to supply under normal operating conditions.

tag_set.value: Design Flow in Million Gallons per Day (MGD)

attributes.name: dma_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the DMA. Active DMAs are fully operational and monitored; inactive or decommissioned DMAs are no longer in use; planned DMAs are in design phase; under review indicates reconfiguration or audit in progress.

attributes.name: established_date
attributes.description: Date when the DMA was first established and commissioned for operational monitoring. Represents the start of the DMAs lifecycle.

attributes.name: gis_polygon_boundary
attributes.description: GIS polygon geometry defining the spatial boundary of the DMA. Typically stored as WKT (Well-Known Text) or reference to GIS layer feature ID for integration with Esri ArcGIS.

attributes.name: inlet_meter_count
attributes.description: Number of inlet flow meters installed at entry points to the DMA. Inlet meters measure total water entering the zone and are critical for NRW calculation.

attributes.name: isolation_valve_count
attributes.description: Number of isolation valves installed at the DMA boundary. Isolation valves enable hydraulic isolation of the DMA for accurate flow measurement and leakage detection.

attributes.name: last_leakage_survey_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent active leakage detection survey conducted within the DMA. Used to track compliance with leakage management programs.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the DMA record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.

attributes.name: leakage_detection_frequency_days
attributes.description: Frequency in days at which active leakage detection surveys are conducted within the DMA. High-risk or high-leakage DMAs may be surveyed more frequently.

attributes.name: main_length_miles
attributes.description: Total length of water mains (distribution pipes) within the DMA measured in miles. Used for calculating leakage per mile of main and infrastructure density metrics.

attributes.name: minimum_night_flow_threshold_gpm
attributes.business_glossary_term: Minimum Night Flow (MNF) Threshold in Gallons per Minute (GPM)
attributes.description: Minimum Night Flow threshold in Gallons per Minute. MNF is the lowest flow rate measured during nighttime hours (typically 2 AM to 4 AM) when legitimate consumption is minimal. Elevated MNF indicates leakage within the DMA.

tag_set.value: Minimum Night Flow (MNF) Threshold in Gallons per Minute (GPM)

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Human-readable name or designation of the DMA, often reflecting geographic location or service area (e.g., 'Downtown West DMA', 'Industrial Park Zone 3').

attributes.name: next_scheduled_survey_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next active leakage detection survey within the DMA. Used for planning and resource allocation.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, historical context, or any additional information relevant to the DMA management and monitoring.

attributes.name: outlet_meter_count
attributes.description: Number of outlet flow meters installed at exit points from the DMA. Outlet meters are used in complex DMA configurations where water may flow to adjacent zones.

attributes.name: population_served
attributes.description: Estimated population served by the DMA. Used for per-capita consumption analysis and demand forecasting.

attributes.name: prv_count
attributes.description: Number of Pressure Reducing Valves installed within or at the boundary of the DMA. PRVs control pressure to reduce leakage and pipe stress.

attributes.name: scada_monitored_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Monitored Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the DMA is actively monitored by the SCADA system. SCADA-monitored DMAs provide real-time flow, pressure, and alarm data for proactive leakage management.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Monitored Flag

attributes.name: service_connection_count
attributes.description: Total number of active service connections (customer meters) within the DMA. Used for calculating per-connection leakage rates and NRW metrics.

attributes.name: target_nrw_percentage
attributes.description: Target threshold for Non-Revenue Water as a percentage of total water supplied to the DMA. NRW includes physical losses (leakage) and commercial losses (metering inaccuracies, theft). Typical industry targets range from 10% to 20%.

attributes.name: target_ufw_percentage
attributes.description: Target threshold for Unaccounted-for Water as a percentage of total water supplied. UFW is a broader measure than NRW and includes all water that cannot be accounted for through billing or authorized use.

products.name: network_valve
products.description: Master record for all distribution network valves including gate valves, butterfly valves, ball valves, and check valves used for flow control and system isolation. Captures valve type, size (inches), manufacturer, installation year, location (GIS coordinates, street address, nearest main reference), pressure zone, DMA, normal operating position (open/closed), number of turns to operate, last exercised date, condition rating, and CMMS asset reference. Critical for isolation analysis (determining minimum valve closures to isolate a pipe segment) and valve exercising programs.
products.reference: AWWA C500/C504/C507 valve standards; IBM Maximo asset record

attributes.name: network_valve_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the distribution network valve record. Primary key.

attributes.name: dma_id
attributes.description: Reference to the District Metered Area (DMA) containing this valve. DMAs are isolated network sections with defined boundaries and metered inflows/outflows, used for Non-Revenue Water (NRW) and Unaccounted-for Water (UFW) analysis. Valves on DMA boundaries are critical for isolation and flow control.

attributes.name: pipe_main_id
attributes.description: Reference to the water main on which this valve is installed. Links the valve to the pipe segment for network topology modeling in Esri ArcGIS and Innovyze InfoWater.

attributes.name: pressure_zone_id
attributes.description: Reference to the pressure zone in which this valve is located. Pressure zones are geographic areas maintained at specific pressure ranges (measured in Pounds per Square Inch - PSI) to ensure adequate service and prevent pipe bursts. Critical for hydraulic modeling in Innovyze InfoWater.

attributes.name: primary_network_asset_registry_id
attributes.description: Reference to the asset registry record in the Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS). Links this valve to IBM Maximo Asset Management for maintenance tracking, work orders, and lifecycle management.

attributes.name: burial_depth_feet
attributes.description: Depth in feet from ground surface to the valve operating nut. Used for excavation planning, valve box sizing, and accessibility assessment. Typical range is 3-8 feet depending on frost line and main depth.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City or municipality in which the valve is located. Used for jurisdictional reporting and geographic analysis.

attributes.name: condition_rating
attributes.description: Assessment of the valve's physical condition based on the most recent inspection. Excellent: like new, no defects; Good: minor wear, fully functional; Fair: moderate wear, functional with minor issues; Poor: significant deterioration, may fail soon; Critical: immediate replacement required. Used for risk-based asset management and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) planning.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this valve record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and compliance with data governance policies.

attributes.name: criticality_rating
attributes.description: Business criticality of the valve based on its role in the distribution network. Critical valves control large trunk mains or serve hospitals/fire protection; high criticality valves isolate major zones; medium and low criticality valves serve smaller areas. Drives maintenance prioritization and replacement sequencing.

attributes.name: current_position
attributes.description: Actual current position of the valve as of the last field verification or SCADA reading. May differ from normal_position during maintenance, emergencies, or operational adjustments.

attributes.name: diameter_inches
attributes.description: Nominal diameter of the valve in inches. Critical for hydraulic modeling in Innovyze InfoWater and flow capacity calculations. Common sizes range from 2 to 48 inches in distribution networks.

attributes.name: exercising_frequency_months
attributes.description: Planned frequency in months for valve exercising activities. Typically 12 months for standard valves, 6 months for critical isolation valves, and 24 months for low-priority valves. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling in CMMS.

attributes.name: gis_feature_code
attributes.description: Unique feature identifier in the Esri ArcGIS system. Links this valve record to the corresponding GIS feature layer for spatial analysis, map display, and network topology modeling.

attributes.name: installation_date
attributes.description: Date the valve was originally installed in the distribution network. Used for age-based asset management, depreciation calculations, and replacement planning under the Capital Improvement Program (CIP).

attributes.name: installation_year
attributes.description: Year the valve was installed. Derived from installation_date for simplified age analysis and reporting when exact date is not required.

attributes.name: is_buried
attributes.description: Indicates whether the valve is buried underground (True) or above ground in a vault or building (False). Buried valves require valve box access and are more difficult to exercise; above-ground valves are more accessible but require weather protection.

attributes.name: is_motorized
attributes.description: Indicates whether the valve is equipped with a motor or actuator for remote operation. True for SCADA-controlled valves; False for manual valves requiring field crew operation.

attributes.name: last_exercised_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the crew member or contractor who last exercised the valve. Supports accountability and quality assurance in valve maintenance programs.

attributes.name: last_exercised_date
attributes.description: Date the valve was last exercised (opened and closed through its full range of motion). Regular valve exercising prevents seizing and ensures operability during emergencies. AWWA recommends annual exercising for critical valves.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the valve location in decimal degrees. Used for Geographic Information System (GIS) mapping in Esri ArcGIS, field crew navigation, and spatial analysis.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the valve location in decimal degrees. Used for Geographic Information System (GIS) mapping in Esri ArcGIS, field crew navigation, and spatial analysis.

attributes.name: material
attributes.description: Primary construction material of the valve body. Ductile iron is most common for large distribution valves; bronze and brass for smaller service valves; stainless steel for corrosive environments; PVC for low-pressure applications.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this valve record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit requirements, and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: normal_position
attributes.description: Standard operating position of the valve under normal conditions. Critical for hydraulic modeling, isolation planning, and Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) monitoring. Most distribution valves are normally open; some isolation and control valves are normally closed or throttled.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, access restrictions, or historical information about the valve. Examples: 'Requires two-person crew due to tight turns', 'Located in private easement, coordinate access', 'Replaced stem in 2018'.

attributes.name: operating_pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Valve Operating Pressure (Pounds per Square Inch - PSI)
attributes.description: Normal operating pressure at the valve location in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI). Used for hydraulic modeling, pressure zone verification, and valve sizing validation. Typical distribution system pressures range from 40-120 PSI.

tag_set.value: Valve Operating Pressure (Pounds per Square Inch - PSI)

attributes.name: operational_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the valve in the distribution network. Active valves are in service; inactive valves are temporarily out of service; abandoned valves are no longer used but not removed; removed valves have been physically extracted; planned valves are scheduled for installation.

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal code of the valve location. Supports geographic segmentation and service area analysis.

attributes.name: pressure_rating_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Valve Pressure Rating (Pounds per Square Inch - PSI)
attributes.description: Maximum rated working pressure of the valve in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) as specified by the manufacturer. Must exceed operating_pressure_psi with adequate safety margin. Common ratings are 150, 200, 250, and 300 PSI.

tag_set.value: Valve Pressure Rating (Pounds per Square Inch - PSI)

attributes.name: scada_tag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag
attributes.description: SCADA system tag identifier for automated valves with remote monitoring and control capability. Links to OSIsoft PI Historian for real-time position monitoring, alarm management, and operational analytics. Only populated for motorized or actuated valves integrated with SCADA.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: State or province in which the valve is located. Used for regulatory reporting to State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies.

attributes.name: street_address
attributes.description: Nearest street address or intersection to the valve location. Provides human-readable location reference for field crews, emergency responders, and customer service representatives.

attributes.name: turns_to_close
attributes.description: Number of complete turns required to fully close the valve from the fully open position. Used by field crews during valve exercising programs and emergency isolation procedures. Typical range is 5-50 turns depending on valve size and type.

attributes.name: useful_life_years
attributes.description: Expected useful life of the valve in years from installation date. Used for depreciation calculations per Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB) standards, and for long-term replacement planning. Typical range is 50-75 years for distribution valves.

attributes.name: valve_box_type
attributes.description: Type of valve box or access structure protecting the buried valve. Standard boxes for sidewalk/lawn areas; traffic-rated boxes for roadways; extension boxes for deep valves; vaults for large valves; none for above-ground installations.

attributes.name: valve_function
attributes.description: Primary operational function of the valve in the distribution network. Isolation valves segment the network for maintenance; control valves regulate flow; pressure reducing valves (PRV) manage pressure zones; check valves prevent backflow; air release valves expel trapped air; blowoff valves drain sections.

attributes.name: valve_number
attributes.description: Externally-known business identifier for the valve, typically painted or tagged on the valve in the field. Used by operations and maintenance crews for identification.

attributes.name: valve_type
attributes.description: Classification of the valve mechanism. Gate valves provide full flow with minimal pressure drop; butterfly valves are compact and quick-operating; ball valves offer tight shutoff; check valves prevent backflow; plug, cone, and needle valves provide throttling control. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gate|butterfly|ball|check|plug|cone|needle — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

products.name: hydrant
products.description: Master record for fire hydrants in the distribution network. Captures hydrant type (dry barrel, wet barrel), manufacturer, installation year, GIS location, nearest main pipe, outlet size and count, color coding (flow capacity class per NFPA 291), last flow test date, last inspection date, condition status, and municipality ownership flag. Supports fire flow planning, flushing programs, and regulatory compliance.
products.reference: NFPA 291 Fire Flow Testing; AWWA M17 Installation, Field Testing, and Maintenance of Fire Hydrants

attributes.name: hydrant_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the fire hydrant asset in the distribution network. Primary key.

attributes.name: dma_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the District Metered Area (DMA) to which the hydrant belongs, used for Non-Revenue Water (NRW) analysis and leak detection programs.

attributes.name: hydrant_main_pipe_pipe_main_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the nearest or connected water main pipe from which the hydrant is fed, used for hydraulic modeling and network topology analysis.

attributes.name: hydrant_pipe_main_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the nearest or connected water main pipe from which the hydrant is fed, used for hydraulic modeling and network topology analysis.

attributes.name: isolation_valve_network_valve_id
attributes.business_glossary_term: Isolation Valve Network Valve Id (Foreign Key)
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to distribution.network_valve. Business justification: Every fire hydrant in a water distribution network has an associated isolation valve (the hydrant control valve) that is a distinct asset tracked in the network_valve table. Linking hydrant to its iso

tag_set.value: Isolation Valve Network Valve Id (Foreign Key)

attributes.name: pressure_zone_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the pressure zone or hydraulic district in which the hydrant is located, used for pressure management and network segmentation.

attributes.name: registry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Hydrants are individual assets with inspection schedules, maintenance requirements, replacement cost tracking, and regulatory compliance needs (NFPA/ISO). Essential for asset lifecycle management and

attributes.name: bury_depth_feet
attributes.description: Depth in feet from ground surface to the hydrant valve or base, critical for freeze protection and installation specifications.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City or municipality name where the hydrant is located.

attributes.name: condition_status
attributes.description: Current physical condition assessment of the hydrant based on inspection findings: excellent (like new), good (minor wear), fair (functional with moderate wear), poor (requires repair), critical (non-functional or unsafe).

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this hydrant record was first created in the asset management system.

attributes.name: criticality_rating
attributes.description: Business criticality rating based on consequence-of-failure analysis: critical (hospitals, schools, high-density areas), high (commercial districts), medium (residential), low (rural or redundant coverage).

attributes.name: fire_district
attributes.description: Name or code of the fire protection district or fire department jurisdiction responsible for this hydrant, used for emergency response coordination.

attributes.name: flow_capacity_gpm
attributes.business_glossary_term: Fire Flow Capacity in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)
attributes.description: Rated fire flow capacity of the hydrant in gallons per minute (GPM) at 20 pounds per square inch (PSI) residual pressure, determined by flow testing per NFPA 291.

tag_set.value: Fire Flow Capacity in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)

attributes.name: flow_class_color
attributes.business_glossary_term: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Flow Class Color Code
attributes.description: Color coding per NFPA 291 indicating fire flow capacity class: Red (<500 GPM), Orange (500-999 GPM), Green (1000-1499 GPM), Blue (>=1500 GPM), Light Blue (>=2500 GPM). Used for visual identification by fire departments.

tag_set.value: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) Flow Class Color Code

attributes.name: flushing_program_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this hydrant is included in the routine unidirectional flushing program for water quality maintenance and sediment removal.

attributes.name: gis_feature_code
attributes.description: Unique feature identifier from the Esri ArcGIS system linking this hydrant record to the spatial GIS layer for network modeling and map visualization.

attributes.name: hydrant_number
attributes.description: External business identifier or asset tag number assigned to the hydrant for field operations, maintenance tracking, and municipal records.

attributes.name: hydrant_type
attributes.description: Classification of hydrant design. Dry barrel hydrants drain after use (freeze-resistant for cold climates), wet barrel hydrants remain charged with water (warm climates), flush hydrants are below-grade, wall hydrants are building-mounted.

attributes.name: installation_date
attributes.description: Date when the hydrant was originally installed in the distribution network.

attributes.name: installation_year
attributes.description: Year of hydrant installation, used for age-based asset management, depreciation schedules, and replacement planning.

attributes.name: last_flow_test_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent fire flow test conducted on the hydrant per NFPA 291 standards, used to verify flow capacity and pressure performance.

attributes.name: last_flushing_date
attributes.description: Date when the hydrant was last used for system flushing or water quality maintenance activities.

attributes.name: last_inspection_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent routine inspection of the hydrant for physical condition, operability, and maintenance needs.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this hydrant record was last updated in the asset management system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the hydrant location for GIS mapping, spatial analysis, and emergency response routing.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the hydrant location for GIS mapping, spatial analysis, and emergency response routing.

attributes.name: main_diameter_inches
attributes.description: Diameter of the connected water main pipe in inches, critical for fire flow capacity calculations and hydraulic modeling.

attributes.name: next_inspection_due_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next routine inspection of the hydrant, based on preventive maintenance (PM) schedule and regulatory requirements.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special instructions related to the hydrant (e.g., access restrictions, historical issues, special maintenance requirements).

attributes.name: operational_status
attributes.description: Current operational status of the hydrant in the distribution network: in_service (active and available), out_of_service (temporarily unavailable), under_repair (maintenance in progress), abandoned (permanently removed from service), planned (not yet installed).

attributes.name: outlet_count
attributes.description: Total number of discharge outlets (nozzles) on the hydrant, typically 2-5 outlets including pumper and hose connections.

attributes.name: outlet_size_inches
attributes.description: Sizes of hydrant outlets in inches, typically formatted as a comma-separated list (e.g., '2.5,2.5,4.5' for two 2.5-inch hose outlets and one 4.5-inch pumper outlet).

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Entity responsible for ownership and maintenance of the hydrant: utility_owned (water utility), municipality_owned (city/town), private (property owner), fire_district (fire protection district).

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the hydrant location, used for geographic segmentation and service area analysis.

attributes.name: residual_pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Residual Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)
attributes.description: Residual water pressure at the hydrant in pounds per square inch (PSI) during flow testing at rated capacity, used to assess available fire flow.

tag_set.value: Residual Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)

attributes.name: scada_tag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag Identifier
attributes.description: SCADA system tag or point identifier if the hydrant is equipped with remote monitoring sensors (e.g., pressure transducers), integrated with OSIsoft PI Historian.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag Identifier

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: State or province code (e.g., CA, TX, ON) where the hydrant is located.

attributes.name: static_pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Static Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)
attributes.description: Static water pressure at the hydrant location in pounds per square inch (PSI) when no water is flowing, measured during flow testing.

tag_set.value: Static Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)

attributes.name: street_address
attributes.description: Street address or nearest intersection where the hydrant is located, used for field crew dispatch and fire department coordination.

attributes.name: valve_turns_to_open
attributes.business_glossary_term: Valve Turns Required to Fully Open
attributes.description: Number of complete turns required to fully open the hydrant main valve, used for operational training and maintenance documentation.

tag_set.value: Valve Turns Required to Fully Open

products.name: pump_station
products.description: Master record for booster pump stations within the distribution network that maintain pressure and flow across the system. Captures station name, location (GIS), pressure zone served, design flow capacity (GPM/MGD), total dynamic head (TDH), number of pumps, VFD configuration, SCADA integration tags (OSIsoft PI Historian), power supply details, backup generator status, and operational status. Distinct from WTP/WWTP pump stations owned by treatment/wastewater domains.
products.reference: AWWA M44; OSIsoft PI Historian station tag group

attributes.name: pump_station_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the booster pump station within the distribution network. Primary key.

attributes.name: compliance_permit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Pump stations in water utilities require individual operating permits (state operating permits, air quality permits for backup generators, NPDES discharge permits). Regulatory compliance tracking for

attributes.name: dma_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the District Metered Area (DMA) that this pump station serves for NRW and UFW analysis.

attributes.name: pressure_zone_id
attributes.description: Identifier of the pressure zone served by this pump station for hydraulic modeling and network segmentation.

attributes.name: registry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Pump stations are critical assets requiring comprehensive lifecycle management, PM scheduling, condition assessments, criticality ratings, and depreciation tracking. Core requirement for enterprise as

attributes.name: source_facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to treatment.facility. Business justification: Pump stations receive finished water from a specific treatment facility. Hydraulic modeling, operational dispatch, and water quality compliance (chlorine residual tracking through the distribution sys

attributes.name: address_line_1
attributes.description: Primary street address of the pump station facility for physical access and emergency response.

attributes.name: address_line_2
attributes.description: Secondary address information such as building number or suite for the pump station facility.

attributes.name: asset_condition_rating
attributes.description: Current condition assessment rating of the pump station based on inspection and maintenance records.

attributes.name: backup_generator_available
attributes.description: Indicates whether the pump station has a backup generator for emergency power supply.

attributes.name: backup_generator_capacity_kw
attributes.description: Capacity of the backup generator in kilowatts (kW) if available.

attributes.name: city
attributes.description: City or municipality where the pump station is located.

attributes.name: country_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO country code where the pump station is located.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this pump station record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: criticality_rating
attributes.description: Business criticality rating indicating the importance of this pump station to system operations and service delivery.

attributes.name: design_flow_capacity_gpm
attributes.business_glossary_term: Design Flow Capacity in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)
attributes.description: Maximum design flow capacity of the pump station measured in gallons per minute (GPM).

tag_set.value: Design Flow Capacity in Gallons Per Minute (GPM)

attributes.name: design_flow_capacity_mgd
attributes.business_glossary_term: Design Flow Capacity in Million Gallons Per Day (MGD)
attributes.description: Maximum design flow capacity of the pump station measured in million gallons per day (MGD).

tag_set.value: Design Flow Capacity in Million Gallons Per Day (MGD)

attributes.name: discharge_pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Discharge Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)
attributes.description: Target discharge pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) maintained by the pump station.

tag_set.value: Discharge Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)

attributes.name: gis_feature_code
attributes.description: Unique feature identifier in the Esri ArcGIS system linking this pump station to the GIS network model.

attributes.name: hydraulic_model_node_code
attributes.description: Node identifier in the Innovyze InfoWater hydraulic model representing this pump station.

attributes.name: installation_date
attributes.description: Date when the pump station was originally installed and commissioned.

attributes.name: last_major_upgrade_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent major upgrade or rehabilitation of the pump station.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this pump station record was last updated in the system.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the pump station location in decimal degrees for GIS integration.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the pump station location in decimal degrees for GIS integration.

attributes.name: maximo_asset_number
attributes.description: Asset number assigned to the pump station in the IBM Maximo CMMS for maintenance management.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or historical information about the pump station.

attributes.name: number_of_duty_pumps
attributes.description: Count of pumps designated for normal operational duty at the station.

attributes.name: number_of_pumps
attributes.description: Total count of pump units installed at the station, including duty and standby pumps.

attributes.name: number_of_standby_pumps
attributes.description: Count of pumps designated as standby or backup units for redundancy.

attributes.name: operational_status
attributes.description: Current operational state of the pump station indicating availability for service.

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Ownership classification of the pump station asset.

attributes.name: postal_code
attributes.description: Postal or ZIP code for the pump station location.

attributes.name: power_supply_phase
attributes.description: Electrical power supply phase configuration for the pump station.

attributes.name: power_supply_voltage
attributes.description: Electrical power supply voltage specification for the pump station (e.g., 480V, 4160V).

attributes.name: scada_integrated
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Integrated
attributes.description: Indicates whether the pump station is integrated with the SCADA system for remote monitoring and control.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Integrated

attributes.name: scada_tag_prefix
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag Prefix
attributes.description: Prefix used for SCADA tags associated with this pump station in the OSIsoft PI Historian system.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag Prefix

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: State or province code where the pump station is located.

attributes.name: station_code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the pump station for asset tracking and SCADA integration.

attributes.name: station_name
attributes.description: Business name or designation of the pump station for operational reference and reporting.

attributes.name: station_type
attributes.description: Classification of the pump station based on its operational function within the distribution network.

attributes.name: suction_pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Suction Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)
attributes.description: Inlet or suction pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) at the pump station intake.

tag_set.value: Suction Pressure in Pounds Per Square Inch (PSI)

attributes.name: total_dynamic_head_ft
attributes.description: Total dynamic head (TDH) in feet that the pump station must overcome, including elevation and friction losses.

attributes.name: vfd_configuration
attributes.description: Description of the VFD configuration including number of drives and control strategy.

attributes.name: vfd_equipped
attributes.description: Indicates whether the pump station is equipped with Variable Frequency Drive (VFD) technology for flow and pressure control.

products.name: storage_tank
products.description: Master record for potable water storage facilities in the distribution network including elevated tanks, ground-level reservoirs, and standpipes. Captures tank type, material, capacity (gallons/MG), operating level range (min/max feet), overflow elevation, pressure zone served, GIS location, SCADA level sensor tags (OSIsoft PI Historian), last inspection date, coating condition, and regulatory inspection status. Supports system storage adequacy and fire flow reserve analysis.
products.reference: AWWA D100/D103/D110 tank standards; AWWA M42 Steel Water-Storage Facilities

attributes.name: storage_tank_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the potable water storage facility. Primary key for the storage tank master record.

attributes.name: compliance_permit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Storage tanks (elevated tanks, ground reservoirs) require regulatory operating permits and are subject to sanitary survey compliance. State regulators issue permits governing tank inspection frequency

attributes.name: pressure_zone_id
attributes.description: Reference to the pressure zone served by this storage tank. Pressure zones are geographic areas of the distribution network maintained at similar hydraulic pressure ranges.

attributes.name: primary_storage_district_metered_area_dma_id
attributes.description: Reference to the District Metered Area (DMA) that this storage tank serves. DMAs are discrete zones used for water balance analysis and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) management.

attributes.name: registry_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Storage tanks are major capital assets with regulatory inspection requirements (AWWA D100), condition assessments, coating schedules, and depreciation tracking. Essential for asset management and fina

attributes.name: source_facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to treatment.facility. Business justification: Storage tanks hold finished water produced by a specific treatment facility. SWTR and LCR regulatory compliance, CT residual tracking, and water quality reporting require tracing stored water back to

attributes.name: asset_criticality_rating
attributes.description: Risk-based criticality classification of the storage tank based on consequence of failure analysis considering population served, redundancy, and system impact: critical (single point of failure for large population), high (significant impact), medium (moderate impact), or low (minimal impact with redundancy available).

attributes.name: base_elevation_feet
attributes.description: Ground or foundation elevation (in feet above mean sea level or local datum) at the base of the storage tank structure. Used for hydraulic gradient calculations.

attributes.name: capacity_gallons
attributes.description: Total storage capacity of the tank measured in gallons. Represents the maximum volume of potable water the tank can hold at overflow elevation.

attributes.name: capacity_million_gallons
attributes.description: Total storage capacity expressed in million gallons (MG), the standard unit for water utility storage reporting and system adequacy analysis.

attributes.name: coating_condition
attributes.description: Assessment of the current condition of the tank's protective coating system based on the most recent inspection: excellent (no defects), good (minor wear), fair (localized deterioration), poor (widespread deterioration), or failed (coating breakdown requiring immediate attention).

attributes.name: emergency_storage_gallons
attributes.description: Volume of water (in gallons) reserved for emergency supply during system outages, treatment plant failures, or other contingencies. Separate from fire flow reserve and operational storage.

attributes.name: fire_flow_reserve_gallons
attributes.description: Volume of water (in gallons) reserved in the storage tank to meet fire protection requirements and emergency fire flow demands as defined by local fire codes and insurance standards.

attributes.name: gis_feature_code
attributes.description: Unique feature identifier from the Esri ArcGIS system linking this storage tank record to its spatial representation in the GIS network model.

attributes.name: hydraulic_model_node_code
attributes.description: Node identifier in the Innovyze InfoWater hydraulic model representing this storage tank. Used for network simulation, pressure analysis, and system optimization studies.

attributes.name: inlet_pipe_diameter_inches
attributes.description: Diameter (in inches) of the primary inlet pipe supplying water to the storage tank. Used for hydraulic modeling and flow capacity analysis.

attributes.name: installation_date
attributes.description: Date when the storage tank was originally constructed and placed into service. Used for asset age calculation and depreciation schedules.

attributes.name: last_cleaning_date
attributes.description: Date when the storage tank interior was last drained, cleaned, and disinfected. Regular cleaning is required to maintain water quality and prevent sediment accumulation.

attributes.name: last_coating_date
attributes.description: Date when the interior or exterior protective coating was last applied or rehabilitated. Coating maintenance is critical for corrosion prevention and structural longevity.

attributes.name: last_inspection_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent comprehensive inspection of the storage tank, including structural integrity, coating condition, and safety systems. Required for regulatory compliance and asset management.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the storage tank location. Used for GIS mapping, spatial analysis, and integration with Esri ArcGIS.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the storage tank location. Used for GIS mapping, spatial analysis, and integration with Esri ArcGIS.

attributes.name: maximo_asset_number
attributes.description: Asset identifier from IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS) system linking this storage tank to its maintenance history, work orders, preventive maintenance schedules, and spare parts inventory.

attributes.name: maximum_operating_level_feet
attributes.description: Highest water level (in feet) at which the tank should operate under normal conditions, typically set below overflow elevation to provide freeboard and prevent overflow events.

attributes.name: minimum_operating_level_feet
attributes.description: Lowest water level (in feet) at which the tank should operate under normal conditions to maintain adequate system pressure and prevent pump cavitation or structural stress.

attributes.name: mixing_system_installed
attributes.description: Indicates whether an active mixing system is installed in the storage tank to prevent water age stratification and maintain disinfectant residual throughout the tank volume.

attributes.name: mixing_system_type
attributes.description: Type of mixing system installed: mechanical (motor-driven mixer), hydraulic (jet mixing using inlet flow), or none (no active mixing).

attributes.name: next_inspection_due_date
attributes.description: Scheduled date for the next required comprehensive inspection based on regulatory requirements, manufacturer recommendations, or utility inspection frequency policy.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-form text field for additional operational notes, special conditions, historical information, or other relevant details about the storage tank not captured in structured fields.

attributes.name: operational_status
attributes.description: Current operational state of the storage tank in the distribution network: in-service (actively storing and supplying water), out-of-service (temporarily offline), standby (available but not actively used), under-maintenance (undergoing inspection or repair), or decommissioned (permanently retired).

attributes.name: outlet_pipe_diameter_inches
attributes.description: Diameter (in inches) of the primary outlet pipe distributing water from the storage tank to the distribution network. Used for hydraulic modeling and flow capacity analysis.

attributes.name: overflow_elevation_feet
attributes.description: Elevation (in feet above mean sea level or local datum) at which the tank overflow pipe is located. Represents the absolute maximum water level before overflow discharge occurs.

attributes.name: overflow_pipe_diameter_inches
attributes.description: Diameter (in inches) of the overflow pipe that prevents tank overfilling by discharging excess water when the maximum level is reached.

attributes.name: ownership_type
attributes.description: Legal ownership classification of the storage tank: utility-owned (owned and operated by the water utility), leased (leased from another entity), shared (jointly owned with another utility or municipality), or third-party (owned by external entity with service agreement).

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this storage tank record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this storage tank record was last modified. Used for data lineage, change tracking, and audit trail purposes.

attributes.name: regulatory_inspection_status
attributes.description: Current compliance status with state drinking water program and EPA inspection requirements: compliant (meets all requirements), non-compliant (deficiencies identified), pending-review (inspection submitted awaiting approval), or not-applicable (exempt from inspection requirements).

attributes.name: scada_flow_meter_tag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Flow Meter Tag
attributes.description: OSIsoft PI Historian tag name for the flow meter measuring inflow or outflow (GPM or MGD) from this storage tank. Used for demand analysis and water balance calculations.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Flow Meter Tag

attributes.name: scada_level_sensor_tag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Level Sensor Tag
attributes.description: OSIsoft PI Historian tag name for the real-time water level sensor monitoring this storage tank. Used to retrieve current level, historical trends, and alarm conditions from the SCADA system.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Level Sensor Tag

attributes.name: scada_pressure_sensor_tag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Pressure Sensor Tag
attributes.description: OSIsoft PI Historian tag name for the pressure sensor monitoring outlet pressure (PSI) from this storage tank. Used for hydraulic performance monitoring and pressure zone management.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Pressure Sensor Tag

attributes.name: security_system_installed
attributes.description: Indicates whether physical security systems (fencing, locks, intrusion detection, surveillance cameras) are installed to protect the storage tank from unauthorized access and potential contamination threats.

attributes.name: structural_condition
attributes.description: Overall structural integrity assessment of the storage tank based on the most recent inspection: excellent (no defects), good (minor issues), fair (moderate deterioration), poor (significant deterioration requiring repair), or critical (unsafe condition requiring immediate action).

attributes.name: tank_material
attributes.description: Primary construction material of the storage tank structure: steel (welded or bolted), concrete (cast-in-place or precast), prestressed concrete, composite (steel and concrete), or fiberglass.

attributes.name: tank_name
attributes.description: Common name or designation of the storage tank, often referencing geographic location or service area (e.g., 'Hillside Elevated Tank', 'Downtown Reservoir').

attributes.name: tank_number
attributes.description: Business identifier or asset tag assigned to the storage tank for operational reference and field identification.

attributes.name: tank_type
attributes.description: Classification of storage tank by structural configuration: elevated (water tower), ground-level (surface reservoir), standpipe (tall cylindrical), reservoir (large capacity ground storage), clearwell (treated water storage at WTP), or hydropneumatic (pressure tank).

attributes.name: usable_capacity_gallons
attributes.description: Effective storage capacity available for distribution operations, calculated as the volume between minimum operating level and overflow elevation. Excludes dead storage below minimum operating level.

products.name: flow_reading
products.description: Transactional record of flow measurements (GPM/MGD) captured at DMA inlet/outlet meters, pump station discharge meters, PRV station meters, and bulk transfer points. Sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA telemetry and AMI bulk meters. Captures reading timestamp, measurement point reference, raw flow value, engineering unit, data quality flag, and source system. Foundation for NRW/UFW water balance calculations and hydraulic model calibration.
products.reference: OSIsoft PI Historian time-series event; AWWA M36 Water Audit

attributes.name: flow_reading_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the flow measurement record. Primary key for the flow reading transaction.

attributes.name: dma_id
attributes.description: Reference to the District Metered Area (DMA) associated with this flow measurement. Used for Non-Revenue Water (NRW) and Unaccounted-for Water (UFW) analysis.

attributes.name: meter_id
attributes.description: Reference to the specific flow meter device that captured this reading. Links to asset registry for meter calibration history and maintenance records.

attributes.name: pump_station_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to distribution.pump_station. Business justification: The domain description explicitly states flow_reading captures measurements at pump stations. A pump_station_id FK normalizes this relationship, enabling direct JOIN from a flow reading to the pump st

attributes.name: source_facility_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to treatment.facility. Business justification: Distribution system entry-point flow readings (DMA inlet meters, pump discharge meters) must be linked to the originating treatment facility for water balance calculations, NRW analysis, and regulator

attributes.name: storage_tank_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to distribution.storage_tank. Business justification: The domain description states flow readings are captured at storage tanks (inlet/outlet flow monitoring). A storage_tank_id FK links each flow reading to the specific tank being measured, enabling tan

attributes.name: alarm_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this flow reading triggered an alarm condition in the SCADA system (e.g., flow exceeds threshold, negative flow, meter communication failure).

attributes.name: alarm_type
attributes.description: Classification of the alarm condition if alarm_flag is true. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_flow|low_flow|no_flow|reverse_flow|communication_failure|meter_fault|pressure_deviation|temperature_anomaly|data_gap|validation_failure — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: billing_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this flow reading is used for bulk water billing or wholesale customer invoicing (e.g., inter-utility transfers, industrial bulk customers).

attributes.name: calibration_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent meter calibration prior to this reading. Used to assess measurement reliability and schedule recalibration.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Free-text field for operator notes, validation comments, or explanations of anomalies in the flow reading. Used for audit trail and troubleshooting.

attributes.name: data_quality_flag
attributes.description: Quality indicator for the flow reading. Good = validated measurement, Suspect = questionable but not rejected, Bad = failed validation, Estimated = calculated/interpolated value, Manual = operator-entered reading.

attributes.name: engineering_unit
attributes.description: Unit of measure for the flow reading. GPM = Gallons per Minute, MGD = Million Gallons per Day, CFS = Cubic Feet per Second, LPS = Liters per Second, M3H = Cubic Meters per Hour, M3D = Cubic Meters per Day.

attributes.name: estimated_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the flow value is an estimated or interpolated value rather than a direct meter reading. True when meter communication fails or reading is missing.

attributes.name: estimation_method
attributes.description: Method used to estimate the flow value when direct measurement is unavailable. None indicates a direct measured value.

attributes.name: flow_direction
attributes.description: Direction of water flow at the measurement point. Inflow = water entering the zone/DMA, Outflow = water leaving the zone/DMA, Bidirectional = flow can reverse direction.

attributes.name: flow_value
attributes.description: The raw flow measurement value as captured by the meter. Represents instantaneous flow rate or cumulative volume depending on measurement type. Used with engineering_unit to interpret the measurement.

attributes.name: hydraulic_model_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this flow reading is used for hydraulic model calibration in Innovyze InfoWater or similar distribution network modeling software.

attributes.name: interval_duration_minutes
attributes.description: Time interval in minutes over which the flow measurement was aggregated or averaged. Common values: 15, 30, 60 minutes for SCADA polling intervals.

attributes.name: measurement_type
attributes.description: Classification of the flow measurement: instantaneous (real-time snapshot), cumulative (totalizer reading), average (calculated over interval), peak (maximum in interval), or minimum (lowest in interval).

attributes.name: meter_accuracy_percent
attributes.description: The rated accuracy of the flow meter at the time of this reading, expressed as a percentage. Used to calculate measurement uncertainty for water balance calculations.

attributes.name: nrw_calculation_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether this flow reading is included in Non-Revenue Water (NRW) or Unaccounted-for Water (UFW) balance calculations for the associated DMA.

attributes.name: pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)
attributes.description: Water pressure measurement in PSI at the flow measurement point, captured concurrently with the flow reading. Used for hydraulic model calibration and pressure zone analysis.

tag_set.value: Pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)

attributes.name: reading_timestamp
attributes.description: The precise date and time when the flow measurement was captured by the meter or SCADA system. This is the business event timestamp representing the actual measurement occurrence.

attributes.name: record_created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this flow reading record was first inserted into the data system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.

attributes.name: record_updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when this flow reading record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.

attributes.name: scada_tag_name
attributes.business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag Name
attributes.description: The SCADA system tag or point identifier that sourced this flow reading. Used for traceability back to the PI Historian or SCADA historian database.

tag_set.value: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag Name

attributes.name: temperature_f
attributes.description: Water temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at the measurement point. Used for flow compensation calculations and water quality correlation analysis.

attributes.name: totalizer_reading
attributes.description: Cumulative volume reading from the meter totalizer register. Used to calculate interval consumption by differencing consecutive readings. Typically in gallons or cubic meters.

attributes.name: validated_by
attributes.description: User ID or system process name that performed the validation of this flow reading. Used for audit trail and accountability.

attributes.name: validation_status
attributes.description: Current validation state of the flow reading. Pending = awaiting review, Validated = approved by operator or automated validation, Rejected = failed validation rules, Corrected = manually adjusted after validation.

attributes.name: validation_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the flow reading was validated or reviewed by an operator or automated validation process.

products.name: main_break
products.description: Transactional record of distribution network incidents including main breaks (pipe bursts, joint failures), planned shutdowns, and emergency/planned isolation events. Captures incident type, date/time, location (GIS coordinates, address, pipe main reference), failure mode (circumferential, longitudinal, blowout, joint) for breaks, valves operated with sequence for isolations, customers affected count and service addresses, estimated volume lost (gallons), repair method, repair duration, root cause classification, pressure zone impact, crew supervisor, and restoration confirmation timestamp. SSOT for outage management, customer notification, asset renewal prioritization, and regulatory reporting of service interruptions.
products.reference: AWWA M77 Condition Assessment of Water Mains; AWWA break rate benchmarking

attributes.name: main_break_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the distribution main break event. Primary key for the main break record.

attributes.name: dma_id
attributes.description: Reference to the District Metered Area where the break occurred. Used for NRW (Non-Revenue Water) and UFW (Unaccounted-for Water) analysis.

attributes.name: failure_record_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Main breaks are asset failure events requiring root cause analysis, MTBF/MTTR tracking, failure mode classification, and reliability analysis. Links distribution failures to enterprise failure trackin

attributes.name: pipe_main_id
attributes.description: Reference to the distribution main pipe asset where the break occurred. Links to the distribution main asset registry.

attributes.name: pressure_zone_id
attributes.description: Reference to the pressure zone where the break occurred. Critical for hydraulic modeling and pressure management analysis.

attributes.name: violation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_violation. Business justification: Main breaks that cause pressure loss below 20 psi or contamination events directly generate compliance violations (TCR, pressure adequacy, boil water advisory requirements). main_break.regulatory_repo

attributes.name: water_sample_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Main breaks trigger mandatory bacteriological resampling per Revised Total Coliform Rule (RTCR). Boil water advisories and repeat sampling directly tied to break events. Essential for tracking complia

attributes.name: work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the work order created in the CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) for the repair activity. Links to IBM Maximo Asset Management work order.

attributes.name: affected_zones
attributes.description: Comma-separated list of affected pressure zones or DMAs

attributes.name: boil_water_advisory_issued
attributes.description: Indicates whether a boil water advisory was issued to affected customers due to potential water quality compromise. True if advisory was issued, False otherwise.

attributes.name: break_number
attributes.description: Business identifier for the main break event, typically formatted as MB-YYYYNNNNNN for external reference and reporting.

attributes.name: break_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the main break event: reported, dispatched, in progress, repaired, closed, or deferred.

attributes.name: break_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the main break was first detected or reported. Principal business event timestamp for the break occurrence.

attributes.name: break_type
attributes.description: Classification of the main break failure mode: circumferential crack, longitudinal crack, blowout, joint failure, service line break, or corrosion pinhole.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the main break record was first created in the system. Audit trail timestamp for record creation.

attributes.name: customers_affected_count
attributes.description: Number of customer accounts impacted by service disruption due to the main break.

attributes.name: dispatch_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when field crew was dispatched to the main break location.

attributes.name: expected_restoration_at
attributes.description: Expected service restoration timestamp

attributes.name: gis_feature_code
attributes.description: Reference to the GIS feature identifier in Esri ArcGIS for the main pipe segment where the break occurred.

attributes.name: hydraulic_model_node_code
attributes.description: Reference to the node identifier in Innovyze InfoWater hydraulic model for network analysis and pressure simulation.

attributes.name: installation_year
attributes.description: Year the pipe was originally installed in the distribution network.

attributes.name: location_address
attributes.description: Street address or nearest intersection where the main break occurred. Organizational location data classified as confidential.

attributes.name: location_latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the main break location for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.

attributes.name: location_longitude
attributes.description: Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the main break location for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional observations, special circumstances, or detailed notes about the main break event and repair.

attributes.name: operating_pressure_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: Operating Pressure (PSI - Pounds per Square Inch)
attributes.description: Normal operating pressure in the main at the time of break, measured in PSI (Pounds per Square Inch).

tag_set.value: Operating Pressure (PSI - Pounds per Square Inch)

attributes.name: pipe_age_years
attributes.description: Estimated age of the pipe at the time of break, calculated from installation date to break date, measured in years.

attributes.name: pipe_diameter_inches
attributes.description: Nominal diameter of the failed pipe in inches.

attributes.name: pipe_material
attributes.description: Material composition of the failed pipe: cast iron, ductile iron, PVC (polyvinyl chloride), HDPE (high-density polyethylene), steel, concrete, asbestos cement, or copper. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cast_iron|ductile_iron|pvc|hdpe|steel|concrete|asbestos_cement|copper — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: population_at_risk
attributes.description: Estimated population affected by the main break

attributes.name: priority_level
attributes.description: Priority classification assigned to the main break based on severity, customer impact, and safety considerations: emergency, urgent, high, medium, or low.

attributes.name: regulatory_report_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the main break requires regulatory reporting to EPA, state primacy agency, or Public Utilities Commission. True if reporting is required, False otherwise.

attributes.name: repair_complete_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when repair work was completed and the main was returned to service.

attributes.name: repair_duration_hours
attributes.description: Total duration of the repair activity from start to completion, measured in hours.

attributes.name: repair_method
attributes.description: Method used to repair the main break: clamp, sleeve, pipe replacement, joint repair, valve replacement, or temporary bypass.

attributes.name: repair_start_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when repair work commenced on the main break.

attributes.name: reported_by
attributes.description: Source of the main break report: customer, field crew, SCADA alert, patrol, third party, or internal inspection.

attributes.name: reported_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the main break was officially reported to the utility operations center or SCADA system.

attributes.name: root_cause
attributes.description: Identified root cause of the main break: corrosion, age deterioration, soil movement, freeze-thaw cycle, pressure surge, third-party damage, manufacturing defect, or unknown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrosion|age_deterioration|soil_movement|freeze_thaw|pressure_surge|third_party_damage|manufacturing_defect|unknown — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: soil_condition
attributes.description: Soil condition at the break location: clay, sand, gravel, rock, mixed, corrosive, saturated, or unknown. Influences corrosion rates and pipe stability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clay|sand|gravel|rock|mixed|corrosive|saturated|unknown — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: traffic_impact
attributes.description: Impact of the main break on traffic and road access: none, lane closure, road closure, detour required, or emergency access restricted.

attributes.name: updated_timestamp
attributes.description: Date and time when the main break record was last modified. Audit trail timestamp for record updates.

attributes.name: water_lost_gallons
attributes.description: Estimated volume of water lost during the break event, measured in gallons. Critical for NRW (Non-Revenue Water) and UFW (Unaccounted-for Water) reporting.

attributes.name: weather_condition
attributes.description: Weather condition at the time of the break: normal, freezing, extreme cold, heavy rain, drought, snow, or extreme heat. Relevant for freeze-thaw and soil movement analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal|freezing|extreme_cold|heavy_rain|drought|snow|extreme_heat — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

domains.name: metering
domains.description: Owns all metering infrastructure and consumption data including meter inventory, AMI/AMR device management (Sensus FlexNet), meter reads, interval consumption data, leak detection flags, meter accuracy testing, meter replacement programs, and high usage alerts. Serves as the authoritative source for consumption data feeding billing and NRW/UFW analysis.

products.name: meter
products.description: Master inventory record for every physical meter device deployed across the water and wastewater service territory. Captures meter make, model, size, type (AMI/AMR/manual), serial number, manufacturer, installation date, current status, register type, pulse output factor, maximum flow rate (GPM), meter generation, and communication module type. Includes bulk/compound meters, fire service meters, and all residential/commercial/industrial meter classes. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for meter device identity and specifications within the metering domain.

products.name: installation
products.description: Tracks the physical installation of a meter at a service location, linking a specific meter device to a service address and customer account. Records installation date, installer ID, work order reference, meter position (pit, vault, curb box), setter size, service line material, lock/seal number, initial register reading at installation, and removal date when replaced. Maintains the full history of which meter served which location over time, supporting NRW analysis and billing continuity.
products.reference: AWWA AMI/AMR Deployment Standards
