attributes.name: primary_failure_asset_registry_id
attributes.description: Reference to the physical asset that experienced the failure (equipment, pipe, pump, valve, etc.).

attributes.name: process_unit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to treatment.process_unit. Business justification: Failure records for treatment equipment must reference the failed process_unit to support MTBF analysis per unit, root cause analysis, and regulatory notification when treatment process failures affec

attributes.name: violation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_violation. Business justification: Asset failures (main breaks, treatment failures, SSOs) directly cause or constitute compliance violations. Regulatory reporting requires linking the physical failure event to the resulting compliance

attributes.name: water_sample_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Asset failures (main breaks, tank overflows, pressure loss events) trigger mandatory water quality sampling per regulatory requirements (e.g., bacteriological sampling after pressure loss per TCR/RTCR

attributes.name: work_order_id
attributes.description: Reference to the corrective maintenance work order generated in response to this failure event.

attributes.name: actual_repair_cost
attributes.business_glossary_term: Actual Repair Cost in US Dollars (USD)
attributes.description: Actual total cost incurred to repair or replace the failed asset after work completion.

tag_set.value: Actual Repair Cost in US Dollars (USD)

attributes.name: affected_system
attributes.description: The operational system or process affected by the failure (e.g., high service pump station, chlorination system, SCADA network, distribution main).

attributes.name: corrective_actions_recommended
attributes.description: List of recommended corrective and preventive actions to prevent recurrence of similar failures.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this failure record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: cso_event_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the failure resulted in a Combined Sewer Overflow event requiring regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: customers_affected_count
attributes.description: Count of customer accounts impacted by the failure event (service interruption, pressure loss, water quality issue).

attributes.name: detection_method
attributes.description: Method by which the failure was discovered (SCADA alarm, operator inspection, customer complaint, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scada_alarm|operator_inspection|customer_complaint|scheduled_inspection|predictive_maintenance|routine_testing|emergency_response — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

attributes.name: downtime_duration_hours
attributes.description: Total duration in hours that the asset was out of service due to the failure.

attributes.name: emergency_response_actions
attributes.description: Detailed description of immediate emergency response actions taken to mitigate the failure (e.g., valve isolation, backup system activation, customer notification).

attributes.name: environmental_impact_description
attributes.description: Description of any environmental impact resulting from the failure (e.g., water body contamination, soil contamination, habitat damage).

attributes.name: failure_cause
attributes.description: Identified root cause of the failure (e.g., age-related deterioration, improper installation, inadequate maintenance, design deficiency, operational error, external damage).

attributes.name: failure_criticality_score
attributes.description: Calculated criticality score based on failure severity, service impact, and asset importance (typically 1-100 scale).

attributes.name: failure_date
attributes.description: Calendar date when the asset failure occurred.

attributes.name: failure_effect
attributes.description: Description of the operational impact and consequences of the failure on system performance and service delivery.

attributes.name: failure_mode
attributes.description: Classification of how the asset failed (e.g., mechanical fracture, electrical short, corrosion perforation, seal leakage, bearing seizure, control malfunction).

attributes.name: failure_number
attributes.description: Business-readable unique identifier for the failure event, typically auto-generated by CMMS.

attributes.name: failure_severity
attributes.description: Severity classification of the failure based on service impact, safety risk, and regulatory implications.

attributes.name: failure_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the failure record in the investigation and resolution workflow.

attributes.name: failure_time
attributes.description: Precise date and time when the asset failure was detected or occurred.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this failure record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: mtbf_impact_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) Impact Indicator
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this failure event should be included in MTBF reliability calculations for the asset class.

tag_set.value: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) Impact Indicator

attributes.name: mttr_hours
attributes.business_glossary_term: Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) in Hours
attributes.description: Measured time in hours from failure detection to restoration of asset to full operational status.

tag_set.value: Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) in Hours

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Additional free-text notes, observations, or context related to the failure event.

attributes.name: overflow_volume_gallons
attributes.description: Estimated volume of wastewater overflow in gallons resulting from SSO or CSO event, if applicable.

attributes.name: pressure_drop_psi
attributes.business_glossary_term: System Pressure Drop in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)
attributes.description: Measured drop in system pressure (PSI) resulting from the failure event.

tag_set.value: System Pressure Drop in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)

attributes.name: production_loss_mgd
attributes.business_glossary_term: Production Loss in Million Gallons per Day (MGD)
attributes.description: Estimated water production capacity lost due to the failure, measured in Million Gallons per Day.

tag_set.value: Production Loss in Million Gallons per Day (MGD)

attributes.name: regulatory_notification_date
attributes.description: Date when regulatory agencies were notified of the failure event, if applicable.

attributes.name: regulatory_notification_required_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the failure event requires notification to regulatory agencies (EPA, state primacy agency).

attributes.name: repair_cost_estimate
attributes.business_glossary_term: Estimated Repair Cost in US Dollars (USD)
attributes.description: Estimated total cost to repair or replace the failed asset, including labor, materials, and contractor costs.

tag_set.value: Estimated Repair Cost in US Dollars (USD)

attributes.name: resolution_date
attributes.description: Date when the failure was fully resolved and the asset was restored to normal operation.

attributes.name: root_cause_analysis_completed_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether a formal root cause analysis (RCA) has been completed for this failure event.

attributes.name: root_cause_analysis_findings
attributes.description: Detailed findings and conclusions from the root cause analysis investigation, including contributing factors and systemic issues identified.

attributes.name: service_interruption_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the failure resulted in a complete service interruption to customers.

attributes.name: sso_event_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the failure resulted in a Sanitary Sewer Overflow event requiring regulatory reporting.

attributes.name: warranty_claim_flag
attributes.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the failure is covered under manufacturer or contractor warranty and a claim was filed.

products.name: acquisition
products.description: Records the acquisition of new assets into the utility's asset base, capturing acquisition type (purchase, construction, donation, transfer), acquisition date, purchase order reference, vendor/contractor, acquisition cost, funding source (CAPEX, grant, developer contribution), commissioning date, and initial condition grade. Triggers creation of the asset_registry record and depreciation_schedule. Integrates with SAP MM purchase orders and SAP FI asset capitalization.
products.reference: SAP FI-AA Asset Acquisition / IBM Maximo Asset

attributes.name: acquisition_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the asset acquisition record. Primary key for the asset acquisition data product.

attributes.name: acquisition_asset_registry_id
attributes.description: Reference to the asset being acquired in the asset registry. Links this acquisition to the corresponding asset_registry record.

attributes.name: acquisition_registry_id
attributes.description: Reference to the asset being acquired in the asset registry. Links this acquisition to the corresponding asset_registry record.

attributes.name: class_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: acquisition currently stores asset_class as a STRING column — a denormalized text reference to the asset classification. asset_class is the authoritative reference table for this taxonomy. Adding a pr

attributes.name: compliance_permit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Capital asset acquisitions (new construction, major replacements) require construction or environmental permits before commissioning. Project managers and compliance officers must link each acquisitio

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Identifier for the Water Treatment Plant (WTP), Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP), pump station, or other facility where the asset is located.

attributes.name: work_order_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to asset.work_order. Business justification: acquisition currently stores work_order_num as a STRING — a denormalized text reference to the work order associated with the asset acquisition (typically the installation or commissioning work order)

attributes.name: acquisition_date
attributes.description: Date on which the asset was legally acquired or title transferred to the utility. Used for depreciation start date calculation and asset lifecycle tracking.

attributes.name: acquisition_status
attributes.description: Current status of the acquisition record in the workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through completion and asset registry creation.

attributes.name: acquisition_type
attributes.description: Method by which the asset was acquired into the utility's asset base. Determines accounting treatment and capitalization rules.

attributes.name: approval_date
attributes.description: Date on which the asset acquisition was formally approved by the designated authority. Part of the audit trail.

attributes.name: approved_by
attributes.description: Name or identifier of the manager or authority who approved the asset acquisition. Required for audit trail and governance.

attributes.name: asset_category
attributes.description: Detailed categorization within the asset class such as pump, valve, pipe, tank, or vehicle. Supports asset hierarchy and reporting.

attributes.name: capitalization_threshold_amount
attributes.description: Minimum dollar amount threshold for capitalizing an asset as defined by the utility's accounting policy. Typically ranges from $1,000 to $5,000 for water utilities.

attributes.name: capitalization_threshold_met_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the acquisition cost meets the utility's capitalization threshold for fixed asset recording. Determines if asset is capitalized or expensed.

attributes.name: commissioning_date
attributes.description: Date on which the asset was placed into service and became operational. Marks the start of depreciation for accounting purposes.

attributes.name: contractor_name
attributes.description: Name of the construction contractor who built or installed the asset, applicable for construction acquisition type.

attributes.name: cost
attributes.description: Total cost of acquiring the asset including purchase price, installation, freight, and other capitalizable costs. Basis for depreciation calculation.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the asset acquisition record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.

attributes.name: currency_code
attributes.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost. Typically USD for United States water utilities.

attributes.name: depreciation_method
attributes.description: Accounting method used to depreciate the asset over its useful life. Straight-line is most common for water utility infrastructure.

attributes.name: functional_location
attributes.description: SAP PM functional location code representing the hierarchical location of the asset within the utility's infrastructure. Supports Geographic Information System (GIS) integration.

attributes.name: funding_source
attributes.description: Source of funds used to finance the asset acquisition. Critical for Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) tracking and regulatory rate case filings.

attributes.name: grant_award_num
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the grant award that funded the acquisition, required for grant compliance reporting.

attributes.name: grant_program_name
attributes.description: Name of the federal, state, or local grant program that funded the acquisition, if applicable. Examples include EPA Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) or State Revolving Fund (SRF).

attributes.name: initial_condition_grade
attributes.description: Condition assessment grade assigned at the time of acquisition. Baseline for future condition monitoring and asset health tracking.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the asset acquisition record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

attributes.name: maximo_asset_num
attributes.description: IBM Maximo Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) asset number assigned at acquisition. External system identifier for cross-reference.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, conditions, or details about the asset acquisition. Supports audit trail and historical context.

attributes.name: purchase_order_num
attributes.description: Purchase order number from SAP Materials Management (MM) or procurement system authorizing the asset acquisition. Links to procurement transaction.

attributes.name: salvage_value
attributes.description: Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Used in depreciation calculation for certain methods.

attributes.name: sap_equipment_num
attributes.description: SAP Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Plant Maintenance (PM) equipment number assigned at acquisition. External system identifier for cross-reference.

attributes.name: useful_life_years
attributes.description: Expected useful life of the asset in years for depreciation calculation. Varies by asset class per Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB) guidelines.

attributes.name: warranty_duration_months
attributes.description: Duration of the warranty period in months. Calculated from warranty start and expiry dates for reporting purposes.

attributes.name: warranty_expiry_date
attributes.description: Date on which the manufacturer or contractor warranty period ends. Critical for maintenance planning and defect liability tracking.

attributes.name: warranty_start_date
attributes.description: Date on which the manufacturer or contractor warranty period begins for the acquired asset.

domains.name: quality
domains.description: Water quality monitoring and compliance including sampling schedules, MCL/MCLG tracking, DBP monitoring (THM, HAA5), PFAS testing, turbidity (NTU), pH, BOD, COD, TSS, TDS, TOC analysis, bacteriological testing, CCR preparation, and regulatory compliance reporting. Manages water quality from source through distribution system and wastewater effluent discharge.

products.name: contaminant_limit
products.description: Regulatory and operational limits for each contaminant at each applicable monitoring context (drinking water, effluent discharge, source water). Captures MCL, MCLG, action level, treatment technique standard, permit-specific effluent limit (daily max, monthly average), applicable regulation citation, effective date, superseded date, and jurisdiction (federal, state primacy agency). Enables automated compliance comparison against analytical_result values.
products.reference: EPA MCL Table / NPDES Permit Effluent Limits

products.name: exceedance
products.description: Transactional record of each confirmed MCL, action level, or permit limit exceedance detected from analytical results. Captures exceedance date, contaminant, sampling point, measured value, applicable limit, exceedance magnitude, regulatory notification deadline, public notification requirement flag, corrective action required, and resolution status. This is the primary operational record driving regulatory response workflows and violation tracking. SSOT: References quality.analytical_result as the canonical source for measured values; this entity captures regulatory exceedance events and response actions.
products.reference: SDWA Violation / NPDES Exceedance Record

products.name: lead_copper_result
products.description: Specialized transactional record for Lead and Copper Rule (LCR/LCRR) monitoring at customer tap sampling sites. Captures sampling round (6-month period), customer service line material classification (lead, galvanized, copper, unknown), first-draw sample result (lead ppb, copper ppb), 90th percentile calculation inputs, action level exceedance flag (lead >15 ppb, copper >1300 ppb), tier classification of sampling site, and corrosion control treatment optimization status. Distinct from general analytical_result due to LCRR-specific site selection, tiering, and 90th percentile compliance methodology.
products.reference: EPA Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR) Monitoring Record

attributes.name: lead_copper_result_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the lead and copper sampling result record.

attributes.name: contaminant_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.contaminant. Business justification: Lead and copper results are for specific contaminants (lead or copper). This FK identifies which contaminant the result applies to, enabling proper linkage to contaminant limits and regulatory require

attributes.name: contaminant_limit_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.contaminant_limit. Business justification: Each Lead and Copper Rule (LCR/LCRR) result is evaluated against a specific regulatory action level defined in contaminant_limit. lead_copper_result already captures lead_action_level_exceeded and cop

attributes.name: customer_account_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer account associated with the sampling site premises.

attributes.name: meter_installation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Lead and Copper Rule explicitly requires sampling at customer taps with documented service line materials. Meter installation is the sampling location for tier classification, 90th percentile calculat

attributes.name: premise_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: Premise characteristics (service line material, building age, plumbing configuration) are mandatory for lead/copper site selection, tier classification, and LCRR compliance. Business process: site inv

attributes.name: sampling_point_id
attributes.description: Reference to the customer tap location selected for Lead and Copper Rule monitoring.

attributes.name: sampling_schedule_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.sampling_schedule. Business justification: Lead/copper monitoring follows LCR sampling rounds with specific site selection and frequency requirements. Links result to regulatory schedule for 90th percentile calculation, action level exceedance

attributes.name: service_address_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to customer.service_address. Business justification: Lead/copper sampling requires precise service address tracking for tier site selection, customer notification within 30 days of result, and LCRR compliance documentation. Already has customer_account_

attributes.name: violation_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_violation. Business justification: LCR violation traceability: when lead_action_level_exceeded or copper_action_level_exceeded is true, the result directly triggers a compliance_violation under the Lead and Copper Rule. This FK enables

attributes.name: water_sample_id
attributes.description: Laboratory-assigned unique identifier for the physical water sample collected at the tap.

attributes.name: analysis_date
attributes.description: Date when the laboratory completed the lead and copper analysis.

attributes.name: analysis_method
attributes.description: EPA-approved analytical method used for lead and copper determination (e.g., EPA 200.8 ICP-MS, EPA 200.9 ICP-AES).

attributes.name: copper_action_level_exceeded
attributes.description: Indicates whether the copper result exceeds the EPA action level of 1300 ppb (1.3 mg/L), triggering potential corrosion control treatment requirements.

attributes.name: copper_result_ppb
attributes.description: Measured concentration of copper in the water sample expressed in parts per billion (ppb or µg/L). Used for 90th percentile calculation and action level comparison.

attributes.name: corrosion_control_treatment_status
attributes.description: Status of corrosion control treatment optimization at the time of sampling, indicating whether the water system has optimized corrosion control per LCRR requirements.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this lead and copper result record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: customer_notification_date
attributes.description: Date when the customer was notified of their individual lead and copper sample result.

attributes.name: customer_notification_sent
attributes.description: Indicates whether the customer was notified of their individual sample result as required by LCRR within 30 days of receiving results.

attributes.name: detection_limit_copper_ppb
attributes.business_glossary_term: Copper Method Detection Limit (Parts Per Billion)
attributes.description: Minimum concentration of copper that the analytical method can reliably detect and quantify for this sample.

tag_set.value: Copper Method Detection Limit (Parts Per Billion)

attributes.name: detection_limit_lead_ppb
attributes.business_glossary_term: Lead Method Detection Limit (Parts Per Billion)
attributes.description: Minimum concentration of lead that the analytical method can reliably detect and quantify for this sample.

tag_set.value: Lead Method Detection Limit (Parts Per Billion)

attributes.name: holding_time_compliant
attributes.description: Indicates whether the sample was analyzed within the EPA-required holding time (6 months for metals) from collection to analysis.

attributes.name: included_in_90th_percentile
attributes.description: Indicates whether this result was included in the 90th percentile calculation for LCRR compliance determination. Invalid or QC-failed samples are excluded.

attributes.name: lead_action_level_exceeded
attributes.description: Indicates whether the lead result exceeds the EPA action level of 15 ppb, triggering potential corrosion control treatment requirements.

attributes.name: lead_result_ppb
attributes.description: Measured concentration of lead in the water sample expressed in parts per billion (ppb or µg/L). Used for 90th percentile calculation and action level comparison.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this lead and copper result record was last updated in the system.

attributes.name: quality_control_status
attributes.description: Laboratory quality control status indicating whether the sample result passed all QC checks including blanks, duplicates, and spike recoveries.

attributes.name: regulatory_reporting_status
attributes.description: Status of this result in the regulatory reporting workflow to state primacy agency and EPA.

attributes.name: remarks
attributes.description: Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special circumstances related to the sample collection or analysis.

attributes.name: sample_collection_date
attributes.description: Date when the first-draw water sample was collected at the customer tap.

attributes.name: sample_collection_time
attributes.description: Precise timestamp when the first-draw water sample was collected, including time of day to verify minimum stagnation period compliance.

attributes.name: sample_ph
attributes.description: pH measurement of the water sample at collection, relevant for corrosion control assessment.

attributes.name: sample_preservation_method
attributes.description: Method used to preserve the sample for metals analysis, typically acidification with nitric acid to pH < 2.

attributes.name: sample_temperature_c
attributes.description: Water temperature at the time of sample collection, recorded to document field conditions.

attributes.name: sample_volume_ml
attributes.description: Volume of the first-draw water sample collected, typically 1 liter (1000 mL) per LCRR protocol.

attributes.name: service_line_material
attributes.description: Material composition of the customer service line as classified under LCRR for site selection and tiering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lead|galvanized_requiring_replacement|lead_status_unknown|non_lead|copper|plastic|steel|iron|unknown — promote to reference product]

attributes.name: site_tier
attributes.description: LCRR-mandated tier classification of the sampling site based on service line material and building construction date (Tier 1: lead service lines, Tier 2: lead status unknown, Tier 3: non-lead).

attributes.name: stagnation_time_hours
attributes.description: Duration in hours that water remained stagnant in the service line prior to sample collection. LCRR requires minimum 6-hour stagnation for first-draw samples.

products.name: ccr_period
products.description: Master record for each annual Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) reporting period. Captures report year, water system name, PWSID (Public Water System ID), primacy agency, report preparation status, publication date, distribution method (mailed, posted, electronic), number of customers served, water source summary, detected contaminant summary count, violation summary, and certification submission date to primacy agency. Serves as the organizing entity for all CCR-related quality data aggregation.
products.reference: EPA Consumer Confidence Report Rule (40 CFR Part 141 Subpart O)

attributes.name: ccr_period_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the Consumer Confidence Report period. Primary key.

attributes.name: regulatory_agency_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_agency. Business justification: CCR submission routing: each annual CCR is submitted to a specific regulatory_agency (primacy agency). This FK normalizes the denormalized primacy_agency text field on ccr_period, enabling automated s

attributes.name: regulatory_requirement_id
attributes.description: FK to the governing regulatory requirement, providing the parallel US/EU/other reference path.

attributes.name: regulatory_submission_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: CCR regulatory filing: each ccr_period results in a formal regulatory_submission filed with the primacy agency. This FK enables tracking submission status, acknowledgment receipt, and agency response

attributes.name: water_system_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.water_system. Business justification: A Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) period is produced for a specific water system. ccr_period currently stores water_system_name as a denormalized STRING, but water_system is the authoritative master

attributes.name: certification_method
attributes.description: Method used to submit the CCR certification to the primacy agency. Many states now require electronic submission through online portals.

attributes.name: certification_submission_date
attributes.description: Date when the CCR certification was submitted to the primacy agency. Systems must certify delivery of the CCR to customers by October 1.

attributes.name: certified_by_name
attributes.description: Name of the authorized water system official who certified the CCR. Typically the system manager, superintendent, or designated compliance officer.

attributes.name: certified_by_title
attributes.description: Job title of the authorized official who certified the CCR.

attributes.name: comments
attributes.description: Additional notes, observations, or context about this CCR period, preparation process, or special circumstances.

attributes.name: contact_email
attributes.description: Email address for customer inquiries about the CCR. Increasingly included as an additional contact method.

attributes.name: contact_name
attributes.description: Name of the water system contact person for customer questions about the CCR. Required to be included in the published report.

attributes.name: contact_phone
attributes.description: Phone number for customer inquiries about the CCR. Required to be included in the published report.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this CCR period record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: customers_served_count
attributes.description: Total number of customer accounts or service connections served by the water system during the report year. Used to determine CCR distribution requirements.

attributes.name: detected_contaminant_count
attributes.description: Total number of regulated contaminants detected in the water system during the report year. Includes contaminants above and below Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCL).

attributes.name: distribution_method
attributes.description: Primary method used to deliver the Consumer Confidence Report to customers. EPA allows mail, electronic delivery, or posting with notification.

attributes.name: document_file_path
attributes.description: File system path or cloud storage location of the final published CCR document (typically PDF format).

attributes.name: eu_article_17_compliance_flag
attributes.description: Whether this reporting period meets EU DWD 2020/2184 Article 17 requirements for consumer information and online access to water quality data.

attributes.name: eu_information_access_method
attributes.description: Method by which consumer water quality information is made accessible per EU DWD 2020/2184 Art 14 (e.g. online portal, annual mailing, on-request). NULL for US jurisdiction.

attributes.name: eu_reference_path
attributes.description: EU reference path: Drinking Water Directive 2020/2184; REACH; WFD; UWWTD citation chain.

attributes.name: health_effects_language_included_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether mandatory EPA health effects language was included for all detected contaminants. Required for CCR compliance.

attributes.name: jurisdiction
attributes.description: Jurisdiction flavor for this record. Allowed: US,EU,OTHER.

attributes.name: jurisdiction_code
attributes.description: Regulatory jurisdiction for this CCR/consumer information report (US_EPA, US_STATE, EU_MEMBER_STATE, OTHER). EU equivalent is DWD 2020/2184 Article 17 consumer information provision.

attributes.name: jurisdiction_region
attributes.description: Regulatory region code (US, EU, OTHER) for this consumer confidence report period. US requires annual CCR per SDWA; EU requires equivalent consumer information under Drinking Water Directive 2020/2184 Article 17.

attributes.name: language_accessibility_provided_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the CCR was made available in languages other than English to serve non-English speaking populations. Required for systems serving significant non-English speaking populations.

attributes.name: lead_copper_educational_information_flag
attributes.business_glossary_term: Lead and Copper Educational Information Flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether mandatory lead and copper educational information was included in the CCR. Required under Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR).

tag_set.value: Lead and Copper Educational Information Flag

attributes.name: mcl_violation_count
attributes.description: Number of Maximum Contaminant Level violations that occurred during the report year. Must be prominently disclosed in the CCR.

attributes.name: modified_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this CCR period record.

attributes.name: modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this CCR period record was last modified.

attributes.name: monitoring_violation_count
attributes.description: Number of monitoring and reporting violations that occurred during the report year. Includes failures to test or report as required.

attributes.name: online_ccr_url
attributes.description: Web address where the CCR is posted online. Required if using electronic delivery method.

attributes.name: population_served_count
attributes.description: Estimated total population receiving water from the system during the report year. Used for regulatory classification and reporting.

attributes.name: preparation_start_date
attributes.description: Date when preparation of the Consumer Confidence Report began. Typically starts in January following the report year.

attributes.name: publication_date
attributes.description: Date when the Consumer Confidence Report was published and made available to customers. Must be by July 1 following the report year per EPA regulations.

attributes.name: pwsid
attributes.description: EPA-assigned unique identifier for the public water system. Format: two-letter state code followed by seven digits (e.g., CA1234567).

attributes.name: regulatory_framework
attributes.description: Applicable regulatory framework for the region (US=EPA/AWWA/NPDWR/UCMR/NPDES; EU=Drinking Water Directive 2020/2184; REACH; Water Framework Directive; Urban Wastewater Treatment Directive; OTHER=Other national/regional frameworks).

attributes.name: regulatory_region
attributes.description: Top-level regulatory region (US, EU, OTHER). US requires annual CCR per SDWA; EU requires real-time online access to water quality data per DWD 2020/2184 Article 17. Allowed regions: US,EU,OTHER.

attributes.name: regulatory_region_code
attributes.description: ISO region code identifying the jurisdiction for this consumer confidence / water quality report period (US, EU, OTHER). US uses CCR per SDWA; EU uses DWD 2020/2184 Art 14 consumer information.

attributes.name: report_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the Consumer Confidence Report. Tracks progression from draft through publication and certification to primacy agency.

attributes.name: report_year
attributes.description: Calendar year for which this Consumer Confidence Report is prepared. CCRs are annual reports covering the previous calendar year's water quality data.

attributes.name: source_water_assessment_summary
attributes.description: Summary of the source water assessment including susceptibility to contamination and availability information. Required CCR content element.

attributes.name: special_notices_included
attributes.description: Description of any special notices included in the CCR such as boil water advisories, vulnerable population warnings, or emerging contaminant information.

attributes.name: treatment_technique_violation_count
attributes.description: Number of treatment technique violations that occurred during the report year. Treatment techniques are required processes for contaminants without MCLs.

attributes.name: us_reference_path
attributes.description: US reference path: EPA/AWWA/NPDWR/UCMR/NPDES citation chain.

attributes.name: violation_summary
attributes.description: Narrative summary of all violations that occurred during the report year, including health effects language and corrective actions taken. Required CCR content element.

attributes.name: water_source_summary
attributes.description: Narrative description of the water system's sources including surface water, groundwater, purchased water, and source water protection information. Required CCR content element.

attributes.name: created_by
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who created this CCR period record.

products.name: water_system
products.description: Master reference table for water_system. Referenced by water_system_id.

attributes.name: water_system_id
attributes.description: Primary key for water_system

attributes.name: parent_water_system_id
attributes.description: Self-referencing FK on water_system (parent_water_system_id)

attributes.name: regulatory_agency_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_agency. Business justification: Primacy agency assignment: every water system is regulated by a specific regulatory_agency (primacy agency under SDWA). This FK normalizes the denormalized primacy_agency text field, enabling inspecti

attributes.name: average_daily_consumption_mgd
attributes.description: Average daily water consumption served by the system.

attributes.name: average_daily_production_mgd
attributes.description: Average daily water production volume.

attributes.name: capacity_mgd
attributes.description: Maximum designed water flow capacity.

attributes.name: chlorine_residual_mg_l
attributes.description: Typical residual chlorine concentration.

attributes.name: classification
attributes.description: Ownership and governance classification of the water system.

attributes.name: commissioning_date
attributes.description: Date when the water system entered service.

attributes.name: compliance_status
attributes.description: Current compliance status with water quality regulations.

attributes.name: construction_date
attributes.description: Date when construction of the water system was completed.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when the water system record was created.

attributes.name: decommission_date
attributes.description: Date when the water system was retired, if applicable.

attributes.name: is_active
attributes.description: Indicates whether the water system is currently active in the system.

attributes.name: last_inspection_date
attributes.description: Date of the most recent regulatory inspection.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Geographic latitude coordinate.

attributes.name: location_city
attributes.description: City where the water system is located.

domains.name: compliance
domains.description: Regulatory compliance management including permit tracking (NPDES, IUP, state primacy agency permits), MOR/DMR preparation and submission, violation management, enforcement action tracking, audit trails, environmental reporting, SDWA and CWA compliance, CCR publication tracking, and regulatory correspondence. Ensures adherence to all federal, state, and local water and wastewater regulations.

products.name: enforcement_action
products.description: Formal enforcement action initiated by a regulatory agency (EPA, state primacy agency, NPDES authority) against the utility in response to violations, including Notices of Violation (NOVs), Administrative Orders (AOs), Consent Orders, Compliance Schedules, civil penalties, and criminal referrals. Tracks the issuing agency, action type, associated violations, compliance schedule milestones, penalty amounts, response deadlines, legal counsel assigned, and resolution outcome. Drives the utility's formal regulatory response process.
products.reference: NOV / Administrative Order / Consent Order / Enforcement Action

attributes.name: enforcement_action_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the enforcement action record. Primary key.

attributes.name: compliance_permit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the permit under which the violation and enforcement action occurred (NPDES permit, drinking water permit, IUP, etc.).

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Reference to the water treatment plant (WTP), wastewater treatment plant (WWTP), or other facility subject to the enforcement action.

attributes.name: regulatory_agency_id
attributes.description: FK to agency

attributes.name: violation_id
attributes.description: Reference to the primary violation record that triggered this enforcement action. Links to the violation tracking system.

attributes.name: water_system_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.water_system. Business justification: System-level enforcement tracking: EPA and state enforcement actions under SDWA are taken against water_systems (by PWSID). This FK enables system-level enforcement history, compliance status assessme

attributes.name: wwtp_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to wastewater.wwtp. Business justification: Enforcement actions (NOVs, consent orders, civil penalties) are issued against specific WWTPs for NPDES violations. Direct FK enables enforcement history per WWTP, penalty tracking, and legal response

attributes.name: action_date
attributes.description: Action date

attributes.name: action_number
attributes.description: Official enforcement action number or case number assigned by the regulatory agency (EPA, state primacy agency, NPDES authority). This is the externally-known identifier used in all regulatory correspondence and legal documents.

attributes.name: action_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the enforcement action. Tracks progression from issuance through utility response, negotiation, and final resolution.

attributes.name: action_summary
attributes.description: Brief summary of the enforcement action, including the nature of the violation, regulatory basis, and key requirements. Provides context for reporting and management review.

attributes.name: action_type
attributes.description: Type of formal enforcement action initiated by the regulatory agency. Includes Notice of Violation (NOV), Administrative Order (AO), Consent Order, Compliance Schedule, civil penalty assessment, or criminal referral.

attributes.name: appeal_filed_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the utility filed a formal appeal or contested the enforcement action through administrative or judicial proceedings.

attributes.name: appeal_filing_date
attributes.description: Date the utility filed its appeal or petition for review. Null if no appeal was filed.

attributes.name: board_notification_date
attributes.description: Date the utility's Board of Directors or governing body was notified of the enforcement action. Tracks governance and executive oversight requirements.

attributes.name: civil_penalty_amount
attributes.description: Monetary penalty amount assessed by the regulatory agency in U.S. dollars. Null if no civil penalty was imposed.

attributes.name: compliance_schedule_final_deadline
attributes.description: Final deadline by which all corrective actions and compliance milestones must be completed. Null if no compliance schedule is required.

attributes.name: compliance_schedule_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the enforcement action includes a formal compliance schedule with milestones and deadlines for corrective actions.

attributes.name: corrective_action_required
attributes.description: Description of the corrective actions required by the enforcement action. May include operational changes, infrastructure upgrades, process improvements, or enhanced monitoring.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this enforcement action record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.

attributes.name: document_reference_number
attributes.description: Internal document management system reference number for the enforcement action file. Links to stored correspondence, legal documents, and supporting materials.

attributes.name: issue_date
attributes.description: Date the enforcement action was officially issued or signed by the regulatory agency. This is the principal business event timestamp for the enforcement action lifecycle.

attributes.name: issuing_agency
attributes.description: Name of the regulatory agency that issued the enforcement action (e.g., U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, State Department of Environmental Quality, Regional Water Quality Control Board).

attributes.name: issuing_agency_region
attributes.description: Regional office, district, or jurisdiction of the issuing agency (e.g., EPA Region 5, Northwest Regional Office). Identifies the specific regulatory authority within the agency structure.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: Timestamp when this enforcement action record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.

attributes.name: legal_counsel_assigned
attributes.description: Name of the internal or external legal counsel assigned to manage the utility's response to the enforcement action. Business-confidential information.

attributes.name: legal_firm_name
attributes.description: Name of the external law firm representing the utility, if external counsel is engaged. Null if handled internally.

attributes.name: modified_by_user
attributes.description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this enforcement action record. Audit trail for accountability.

attributes.name: notes
attributes.description: Free-text notes capturing additional context, internal discussions, negotiation history, or other relevant information not captured in structured fields.

attributes.name: penalty_amount
attributes.description: Penalty amount

attributes.name: penalty_paid_amount
attributes.description: Actual amount paid by the utility toward the civil penalty. May differ from assessed amount due to negotiated settlements or payment plans.

attributes.name: penalty_payment_date
attributes.description: Date the civil penalty payment was made to the regulatory agency. Tracks compliance with payment deadlines.

attributes.name: public_notice_date
attributes.description: Date public notice of the enforcement action was issued. Null if public notice is not required.

attributes.name: public_notice_required_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the enforcement action requires public notification under regulatory or consent order terms. Drives public communication and transparency obligations.

attributes.name: received_date
attributes.description: Date the utility officially received the enforcement action notice. Used to calculate response deadlines and compliance timelines.

attributes.name: regulatory_citation
attributes.description: Specific regulatory statute, rule, or permit condition cited in the enforcement action (e.g., Clean Water Act Section 301, 40 CFR 122.41, NPDES Permit Condition III.A.1). Identifies the legal basis for the enforcement.

attributes.name: resolution_date
attributes.description: Date the enforcement action was formally resolved or closed. Indicates completion of all required corrective actions, penalty payments, and regulatory obligations.

attributes.name: resolution_outcome
attributes.description: Final outcome of the enforcement action. Indicates how the action was resolved (compliance achieved, consent order executed, penalty paid, dismissed, or under appeal).

attributes.name: response_due_date
attributes.description: Deadline by which the utility must submit a formal response to the enforcement action. Typically specified in the enforcement notice or calculated based on regulatory timelines.

attributes.name: response_submitted_date
attributes.description: Date the utility submitted its formal response to the enforcement action. Tracks compliance with response deadlines.

attributes.name: sep_description
attributes.description: Description of the Supplemental Environmental Project (SEP) agreed to as part of the enforcement settlement. Null if no SEP is included.

attributes.name: sep_estimated_cost
attributes.description: Estimated cost of the Supplemental Environmental Project (SEP) in U.S. dollars. Used to calculate penalty offsets and track SEP investment.

attributes.name: supplemental_environmental_project_flag
attributes.description: Indicates whether the enforcement action includes a Supplemental Environmental Project (SEP) as part of the settlement. SEPs are environmentally beneficial projects that go beyond compliance requirements and may offset civil penalties.

products.name: regulatory_inspection
products.description: Record of regulatory inspections and audits conducted by EPA, state primacy agencies, or other regulatory bodies at utility facilities. Tracks the inspection type (sanitary survey, compliance inspection, pretreatment audit, NPDES inspection), inspecting agency, facility inspected, inspection date, inspector name, findings and deficiencies identified, significant deficiency classifications, required corrective actions, response deadlines, and final inspection report status. Drives post-inspection corrective action tracking.
products.reference: Sanitary Survey / Compliance Inspection / Regulatory Audit

attributes.description: Foreign key linking to wastewater.wwtp. Business justification: Regulatory agencies conduct compliance inspections at WWTPs under NPDES and pretreatment programs. A direct FK enables inspection history per WWTP, deficiency tracking, and follow-up scheduling — stan

attributes.description: Indicates whether corrective actions are required by the utility in response to inspection findings.

products.name: regulatory_submission
products.description: Master record of all formal regulatory submissions made by the utility to regulatory agencies, serving as the single audit trail for submission compliance. Covers DMR submissions (via NetDMR), MOR submissions, CCR certifications, annual compliance reports, pretreatment program reports, SSO/CSO notifications, PFAS reporting, LCRR service line inventory submissions, and Lead Service Line Replacement (LSLR) progress reports. Tracks submission type, receiving agency, submission date, submission method (NetDMR, state portal, paper, email), reporting period, submitter, certifying official, certification status, acknowledgment receipt, late submission flags, and resubmission history. Enables monitoring of submission timeliness — a key compliance metric tracked by state primacy agencies.
products.reference: Regulatory Filing / Regulatory Report Submission

attributes.name: regulatory_submission_id
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the regulatory submission record. Primary key.

attributes.name: compliance_permit_id
attributes.description: Reference to the permit under which this submission is being made (NPDES permit, IUP, state discharge permit, etc.).

attributes.name: facility_id
attributes.description: Reference to the water or wastewater facility (WTP, WWTP, pumping station, etc.) that this submission pertains to.

attributes.name: original_submission_regulatory_submission_id
attributes.description: Reference to the original submission record if this submission is a correction, amendment, or resubmission.

attributes.name: regulatory_agency_id
attributes.description: Reference to the regulatory agency receiving this submission (EPA, state primacy agency, local health department, etc.).

attributes.name: regulatory_requirement_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Every regulatory submission is made to fulfill a specific regulatory requirement — monthly DMRs fulfill NPDES monitoring requirements, annual CCRs fulfill SDWA consumer confidence report requirements,

attributes.name: water_system_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to quality.water_system. Business justification: PWSID-based submission tracking: regulatory submissions (DMRs, CCRs, annual reports) are filed for specific water_systems identified by PWSID. This FK enables tracking all submissions per water system

attributes.name: wwtp_id
attributes.description: Foreign key linking to wastewater.wwtp. Business justification: WWTPs submit Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMRs) and other NPDES regulatory submissions. Direct FK enables DMR submission history per WWTP, late submission tracking, and agency acknowledgment manageme

attributes.name: acknowledgment_received_date
attributes.description: The date on which the regulatory agency acknowledged receipt of the submission (via NetDMR confirmation, email reply, or other means).

attributes.name: acknowledgment_reference_number
attributes.description: The confirmation or tracking number provided by the regulatory agency upon receipt of the submission.

attributes.name: agency_comments
attributes.description: Comments, feedback, or instructions provided by the regulatory agency regarding the submission.

attributes.name: agency_response_date
attributes.description: The date on which the regulatory agency provided a formal response, feedback, or determination regarding the submission.

attributes.name: agency_response_type
attributes.description: The type of response or action taken by the regulatory agency in response to the submission.

attributes.name: amendment_reason
attributes.description: Explanation of why the submission was amended or corrected, including the nature of the error or omission in the original submission.

attributes.name: certification_date
attributes.description: The date on which the certifying official signed and certified the submission.

attributes.name: created_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this regulatory submission record was first created in the system.

attributes.name: due_date
attributes.description: The regulatory deadline by which this submission must be filed to remain in compliance.

attributes.name: exceedance_count
attributes.description: The number of parameter exceedances (values above permit limits but not necessarily violations) reported in this submission.

attributes.name: is_amendment
attributes.description: Indicates whether this submission is an amendment or correction to a previously submitted report.

attributes.name: is_late_submission
attributes.description: Indicates whether the submission was filed after the regulatory due date, which may trigger enforcement action or violation notices.

attributes.name: last_modified_timestamp
attributes.description: The timestamp when this regulatory submission record was last updated or modified.

attributes.name: noncompliance_explanation
attributes.description: Narrative explanation provided by the utility for any violations, exceedances, or non-compliance events reported in the submission, including corrective actions taken or planned.

attributes.name: public_notice_date
attributes.description: The date on which public notification was issued, if required.

attributes.name: public_notice_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether this submission requires public notification (e.g., CCR publication, SSO public notice, boil water advisory).

attributes.name: reporting_period_end_date
attributes.description: The ending date of the time period covered by this regulatory submission.

attributes.name: reporting_period_start_date
attributes.description: The beginning date of the time period covered by this regulatory submission.

attributes.name: resubmission_required
attributes.description: Indicates whether the regulatory agency has requested a corrected or amended resubmission of the report.

attributes.name: submission_date
attributes.description: The date on which the regulatory submission was formally submitted to the regulatory agency. This is the principal business event timestamp for the submission.

attributes.name: submission_file_format
attributes.description: The file format or media type of the submitted report.

attributes.name: submission_file_path
attributes.description: The file system path or document management system reference to the submitted report document (PDF, XML, CSV, etc.).

attributes.name: submission_method
attributes.description: The method or channel used to submit the report to the regulatory agency. NetDMR is the EPA electronic discharge monitoring reporting system.

attributes.name: submission_notes
attributes.description: Internal notes, comments, or additional context regarding the preparation, submission, or follow-up actions for this regulatory submission.

attributes.name: submission_number
attributes.description: Business identifier or tracking number assigned to the regulatory submission, often used for external reference and correspondence with regulatory agencies.

attributes.name: submission_status
attributes.description: Current lifecycle status of the regulatory submission indicating its progress through the submission and review workflow.

attributes.name: submission_type
attributes.description: Category of regulatory submission. DMR = Discharge Monitoring Report, MOR = Monthly Operating Report, CCR = Consumer Confidence Report, SSO = Sanitary Sewer Overflow, CSO = Combined Sewer Overflow, PFAS = Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances, LCRR = Lead and Copper Rule Revisions. attributes.description: Applicable regulatory framework for the region (US=EPA/AWWA/NPDWR/UCMR/NPDES; EU=Drinking Water Directive 2020/2184; REACH; Water Framework Directive; Urban Wastewater Treatment Directive; OTHER=Other national/regional frameworks).

attributes.name: regulatory_program
attributes.description: Primary regulatory program or framework administered by the agency (e.g., NPDES, SDWA Primacy, Pretreatment, Lead and Copper Rule).

attributes.name: regulatory_region
attributes.description: Regulatory region Allowed regions: US,EU,OTHER.

attributes.name: regulatory_region_code
attributes.description: ISO region code identifying the jurisdiction this agency operates in (US, EU, OTHER). Enables multi-jurisdictional regulatory agency modeling.

attributes.name: reporting_frequency_default
attributes.description: Default or most common reporting frequency required by this agency for compliance reports (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, event-driven, as-required).

attributes.name: reporting_system_name
attributes.description: Name of the electronic reporting system used by the agency (e.g., NetDMR, SDWIS, state-specific portal).

attributes.name: state
attributes.description: Two-letter state or province code where the regulatory agency office is located.

attributes.name: state_province
attributes.description: Two-letter state or province code for the agency's physical mailing address (e.g., WI, IL, CA).

attributes.name: submission_credentials_reference
attributes.description: Reference identifier or location for stored login credentials or API keys used to access the agency's submission portal. Should reference a secure credential vault, not store actual credentials.

attributes.name: submission_portal_name
attributes.description: Name of the online submission portal or system (e.g., NetDMR, State Environmental Portal, OSHA Injury Tracking Application).

attributes.name: submission_portal_url
attributes.description: URL for the online portal or system used to submit compliance reports, permits, or other regulatory documents to this agency (e.g., NetDMR, state e-reporting system).

attributes.name: termination_date
attributes.description: Date when this agency's jurisdiction or regulatory authority over the utility ended or will end. Null if still active.

attributes.name: us_reference_path
attributes.description: US reference path: EPA/AWWA/NPDWR/UCMR/NPDES citation chain.

attributes.name: us_regulatory_framework
attributes.description: US frameworks administered (e.g. EPA,AWWA,NPDWR,UCMR,NPDES).

attributes.name: website_url
attributes.description: Official website URL for the regulatory agency.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`_metrics`.`asset_acquisition`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Capital acquisition KPIs — tracks asset procurement spend, capitalization, warranty coverage, and funding sources to support capital program governance and financial reporting."
  source: "`vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`asset`.`acquisition`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "acquisition_type"
      expr: acquisition_type
      comment: "Type of acquisition (e.g., Purchase, Construction, Donation, Transfer) for capital program categorization."
    - name: "acquisition_status"
      expr: acquisition_status
      comment: "Current status of the acquisition (e.g., Pending, Approved, Commissioned) for capital project pipeline tracking."
    - name: "asset_category"
      expr: asset_category
      comment: "Asset category of the acquired asset for capital spend analysis by infrastructure type."
    - name: "funding_source"
      expr: funding_source
      comment: "Funding source for the acquisition (e.g., Rate Revenue, Grant, Bond) for capital financing analysis."
    - name: "grant_program_name"
      expr: grant_program_name
      comment: "Name of the grant program funding the acquisition, used for grant utilization and compliance reporting."
    - name: "depreciation_method"
      expr: depreciation_method
      comment: "Depreciation method applied to the acquired asset (e.g., Straight-Line, MACRS) for financial reporting segmentation."
    - name: "capitalization_threshold_met_flag"
      expr: capitalization_threshold_met_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the acquisition cost met the capitalization threshold, distinguishing capital from expense items."
    - name: "initial_condition_grade"
      expr: initial_condition_grade
      comment: "Condition grade of the asset at acquisition, used to assess procurement quality and vendor performance."
    - name: "acquisition_year"
      expr: YEAR(acquisition_date)
      comment: "Year of acquisition for annual capital spend trend analysis and CIP program tracking."
    - name: "commissioning_year"
      expr: YEAR(commissioning_date)
      comment: "Year the acquired asset was commissioned into service, used for capital activation lag analysis."
  measures:
    - name: "total_acquisitions"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of asset acquisitions. Baseline KPI for capital program activity volume."
    - name: "total_acquisition_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total capital expenditure across all acquisitions. Primary CIP spend KPI for capital budget management and rate case support."
    - name: "avg_acquisition_cost"
      expr: AVG(CAST(cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average cost per acquisition. Benchmarks unit capital cost across asset categories and funding sources."
    - name: "total_salvage_value"
      expr: SUM(CAST(salvage_value AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated salvage value across acquired assets. Input for net book value calculations and depreciation schedule planning."
    - name: "total_capitalized_acquisitions"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN capitalization_threshold_met_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of acquisitions that met the capitalization threshold. Tracks capital vs. expense classification for financial reporting accuracy."
    - name: "avg_warranty_duration_months"
      expr: AVG(CAST(warranty_duration_months AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average warranty duration in months across acquired assets. Measures warranty coverage quality and vendor contract performance."
    - name: "total_capitalization_threshold_amount"
      expr: SUM(CAST(capitalization_threshold_amount AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Sum of capitalization threshold amounts across acquisitions. Used for financial policy compliance and audit verification."
    - name: "grant_funded_acquisitions"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN grant_program_name IS NOT NULL AND grant_program_name <> '' THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of acquisitions funded through grant programs. Tracks grant utilization effectiveness and external funding leverage for capital programs."
$$
metric_views.description: Capital acquisition KPIs — tracks asset procurement spend, capitalization, warranty coverage, and funding sources to support capital program governance and financial reporting.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`_metrics`.`asset_condition_assessment`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Asset health and risk KPIs derived from condition assessments — supports rehabilitation prioritization, risk-based asset management, and regulatory compliance for infrastructure renewal programs."
  source: "`vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`asset`.`condition_assessment`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "condition_grade"
      expr: condition_grade
      comment: "Condition grade assigned during assessment (e.g., 1–5 or A–F scale) for portfolio health segmentation."
    - name: "assessment_type"
      expr: assessment_type
      comment: "Type of condition assessment performed (e.g., Visual, CCTV, Acoustic) for method effectiveness analysis."
    - name: "assessment_method"
      expr: assessment_method
      comment: "Specific inspection method used, enabling comparison of assessment quality and cost across techniques."
    - name: "assessment_status"
      expr: assessment_status
      comment: "Current status of the assessment record (e.g., Pending, Completed, Reviewed) for workflow management."
    - name: "recommended_action"
      expr: recommended_action
      comment: "Recommended remediation action (e.g., Repair, Replace, Monitor) for capital program planning."
    - name: "recommended_action_priority"
      expr: recommended_action_priority
      comment: "Priority level of the recommended action, used to triage rehabilitation investments."
    - name: "criticality_rating"
      expr: criticality_rating
      comment: "Criticality rating of the assessed asset, enabling risk-weighted condition analysis."
    - name: "regulatory_compliance_flag"
      expr: regulatory_compliance_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the assessment satisfies a regulatory compliance requirement."
    - name: "assessment_year"
      expr: YEAR(assessment_date)
      comment: "Year the condition assessment was performed, used for annual inspection program tracking."
    - name: "assessment_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', assessment_date)
      comment: "Month of assessment for seasonal inspection pattern analysis."
  measures:
    - name: "total_assessments"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of condition assessments performed. Baseline KPI for inspection program throughput."
    - name: "avg_condition_performance_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(performance_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average performance score across assessed assets. Portfolio-level health index for executive reporting and trend monitoring."
    - name: "avg_risk_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(risk_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average risk score across assessed assets. Drives risk-based maintenance prioritization and capital investment decisions."
    - name: "avg_remaining_useful_life_years"
      expr: AVG(CAST(remaining_useful_life_years AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average remaining useful life in years across assessed assets. Core input for long-range capital renewal forecasting."
    - name: "total_estimated_repair_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_repair_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated repair cost across all assessed assets. Quantifies the near-term rehabilitation investment requirement."
    - name: "total_estimated_replacement_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_replacement_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated replacement cost across all assessed assets. Represents the maximum capital exposure for full asset renewal."
    - name: "avg_structural_integrity_score"
      expr: AVG(CAST(structural_integrity_score AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average structural integrity score across assessments. Indicates physical safety and failure risk of the infrastructure portfolio."
    - name: "avg_failure_probability"
      expr: AVG(CAST(failure_probability AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average probability of failure across assessed assets. Key risk metric for prioritizing proactive intervention before service disruption."
    - name: "assets_with_critical_defects"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN recommended_action_priority = 'Critical' THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of assets assessed with critical-priority recommended actions. Drives urgent capital program mobilization."
    - name: "regulatory_compliant_assessments"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN regulatory_compliance_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of assessments that satisfy regulatory compliance requirements. Tracks inspection program compliance rate for regulatory reporting."
$$
metric_views.description: Asset health and risk KPIs derived from condition assessments — supports rehabilitation prioritization, risk-based asset management, and regulatory compliance for infrastructure renewal programs.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`_metrics`.`asset_failure_record`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Asset reliability and failure impact KPIs — measures failure frequency, downtime, cost, and regulatory exposure to drive reliability-centered maintenance and risk mitigation strategies."
  source: "`vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`asset`.`failure_record`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "failure_mode"
      expr: failure_mode
      comment: "Failure mode classification (e.g., Corrosion, Mechanical, Electrical) for root cause pattern analysis."
    - name: "failure_cause"
      expr: failure_cause
      comment: "Root cause of the failure, used to identify systemic issues and inform preventive maintenance strategies."
    - name: "failure_severity"
      expr: failure_severity
      comment: "Severity classification of the failure (e.g., Minor, Major, Critical) for risk prioritization."
    - name: "failure_status"
      expr: failure_status
      comment: "Current resolution status of the failure record (e.g., Open, Resolved) for backlog management."
    - name: "affected_system"
      expr: affected_system
      comment: "Water system component affected by the failure (e.g., Distribution, Treatment, Transmission) for system-level reliability analysis."
    - name: "service_interruption_flag"
      expr: service_interruption_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the failure caused a customer service interruption, linking asset reliability to customer experience."
    - name: "regulatory_notification_required_flag"
      expr: regulatory_notification_required_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the failure triggered a regulatory notification requirement, critical for compliance risk tracking."
    - name: "cso_event_flag"
      expr: cso_event_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the failure resulted in a Combined Sewer Overflow event, a key environmental compliance indicator."
    - name: "sso_event_flag"
      expr: sso_event_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the failure resulted in a Sanitary Sewer Overflow event, a key environmental and regulatory indicator."
    - name: "failure_year"
      expr: YEAR(failure_date)
      comment: "Year the failure occurred, used for annual reliability trend analysis and MTBF benchmarking."
    - name: "failure_month"
      expr: DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', failure_date)
      comment: "Month of failure occurrence for seasonal failure pattern analysis."
  measures:
    - name: "total_failures"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of recorded asset failures. Baseline reliability KPI for trend monitoring and MTBF calculation inputs."
    - name: "total_actual_repair_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(actual_repair_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total actual cost to repair failed assets. Primary unplanned maintenance expenditure KPI for budget impact assessment."
    - name: "avg_actual_repair_cost"
      expr: AVG(CAST(actual_repair_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average repair cost per failure event. Benchmarks unit failure cost across asset types and failure modes."
    - name: "total_downtime_hours"
      expr: SUM(CAST(downtime_duration_hours AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total asset downtime hours resulting from failures. Aggregate service reliability impact KPI for executive reporting."
    - name: "avg_mttr_hours"
      expr: AVG(CAST(mttr_hours AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average Mean Time to Repair in hours. Core reliability engineering KPI measuring maintenance responsiveness and crew efficiency."
    - name: "total_production_loss_mgd"
      expr: SUM(CAST(production_loss_mgd AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total production loss in million gallons per day attributable to failures. Directly quantifies operational capacity impact of asset failures."
    - name: "total_overflow_volume_gallons"
      expr: SUM(CAST(overflow_volume_gallons AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total overflow volume in gallons from CSO/SSO events. Critical environmental compliance and regulatory reporting KPI."
    - name: "service_interruption_failures"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN service_interruption_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of failures that caused customer service interruptions. Directly links asset reliability to customer satisfaction and regulatory compliance."
    - name: "regulatory_notification_failures"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN regulatory_notification_required_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of failures requiring regulatory notification. Tracks regulatory exposure and compliance risk from asset failures."
    - name: "root_cause_analysis_completed"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN root_cause_analysis_completed_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of failures where root cause analysis was completed. Measures quality of failure investigation program and continuous improvement maturity."
    - name: "avg_pressure_drop_psi"
      expr: AVG(CAST(pressure_drop_psi AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average pressure drop in PSI associated with failure events. Hydraulic impact indicator used for system performance and loss analysis."
$$
metric_views.description: Asset reliability and failure impact KPIs — measures failure frequency, downtime, cost, and regulatory exposure to drive reliability-centered maintenance and risk mitigation strategies.

metric_views.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW `vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`_metrics`.`asset_pm_schedule`
WITH METRICS
LANGUAGE YAML
AS $$
  version: 1.1
  comment: "Preventive maintenance program KPIs — measures PM schedule coverage, cost planning, compliance, and labor resource requirements to optimize maintenance strategy and regulatory adherence."
  source: "`vibe_water_utilities_v1`.`asset`.`pm_schedule`"
  dimensions:
    - name: "schedule_status"
      expr: schedule_status
      comment: "Status of the PM schedule (e.g., Active, Inactive, Suspended) for program coverage analysis."
    - name: "work_order_type"
      expr: work_order_type
      comment: "Type of work order generated by the PM schedule (e.g., Preventive, Inspection) for maintenance strategy segmentation."
    - name: "frequency_unit"
      expr: frequency_unit
      comment: "Unit of PM frequency (e.g., Days, Months, Hours) for schedule cadence analysis."
    - name: "trigger_type"
      expr: trigger_type
      comment: "PM trigger type (e.g., Calendar, Meter, Condition-Based) for maintenance strategy maturity assessment."
    - name: "asset_criticality_rating"
      expr: asset_criticality_rating
      comment: "Criticality rating of the asset covered by the PM schedule, enabling risk-weighted PM investment analysis."
    - name: "regulatory_compliance_flag"
      expr: regulatory_compliance_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the PM schedule is required for regulatory compliance, critical for compliance program completeness."
    - name: "seasonal_schedule_flag"
      expr: seasonal_schedule_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the PM schedule is seasonal, used for resource planning and scheduling optimization."
    - name: "downtime_required_flag"
      expr: downtime_required_flag
      comment: "Indicates whether the PM task requires asset downtime, used for outage planning and customer impact minimization."
    - name: "next_due_year"
      expr: YEAR(next_due_date)
      comment: "Year the next PM is due, used for forward-looking workload and budget planning."
    - name: "effective_start_year"
      expr: YEAR(effective_start_date)
      comment: "Year the PM schedule became effective, used for program age and coverage trend analysis."
  measures:
    - name: "total_pm_schedules"
      expr: COUNT(1)
      comment: "Total number of active PM schedules. Baseline KPI for preventive maintenance program coverage and scope."
    - name: "total_estimated_labor_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_labor_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated annual labor cost across all PM schedules. Core input for maintenance budget planning and workforce sizing."
    - name: "total_estimated_material_cost"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_material_cost AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated material cost across all PM schedules. Drives procurement planning and spare parts inventory investment decisions."
    - name: "total_estimated_labor_hours"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_labor_hours AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated labor hours required across all PM schedules. Workforce capacity planning KPI for maintenance resource management."
    - name: "avg_estimated_downtime_hours"
      expr: AVG(CAST(estimated_downtime_hours AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Average estimated downtime per PM task. Used to minimize service disruption through optimized maintenance scheduling."
    - name: "total_estimated_downtime_hours"
      expr: SUM(CAST(estimated_downtime_hours AS DOUBLE))
      comment: "Total estimated downtime hours across all PM schedules. Aggregate planned outage exposure KPI for service reliability planning."
    - name: "regulatory_compliance_schedules"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN regulatory_compliance_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
      comment: "Number of PM schedules tied to regulatory compliance requirements. Tracks mandatory maintenance program completeness for regulatory audits."
    - name: "auto_generate_work_order_schedules"
      expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN auto_generate_work_order_flag = TRUE THEN 1 END)
